articles.title: Ukraine warns of interceptor missile shortage as 18 killed in Kyiv region
articles.summary: President Zelensky says Sunday's "massive Russian attack" on Kyiv consisted of 68 missiles and 351 strike drones.
articles.titleZh: 乌克兰警告拦截导弹短缺 基辅地区18人死亡
articles.summaryZh: 泽连斯基总统表示，周日俄罗斯对基辅发动的“大规模袭击”包括 68 枚导弹和 351 架无人机。

contentParagraphs.en: The Ukrainian Air Force says a "serious shortage" of interceptor missiles meant none of the 23 ballistic missiles fired by Russia at Kyiv on Sunday night were shot down.At least 12 people were killed in the second large-scale Russian attack on the Ukrainian capital in a week, officials said. Six more were killed in the wider Kyiv region.Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has appealed for allies to take "strong decisions" at the this week's Nato summit to provide Kyiv with air defences. After the strikes, he said the Ukrainian military had been successful in intercepting cruise missiles and drones – but not ballistic missiles.Sunday's "massive Russian attack" consisted of 68 missiles and 351 strike drones, he said in a post on X. The air force shot down or suppressed 37 missiles and 326 drones, it said.Zelensky warned that Moscow would continue to hit residential buildings as long as defensive Patriot missiles "remain in our allies' stockpiles".It was another frightening night for people in the capital, with loud explosions and the boom of Ukraine's air defences in action. Widespread destruction was visible on Monday morning. Three large blocks of flats in the city have partially collapsed, some were hit directly by missiles.Helicopters have been shuttling back and forth in the sky, carrying water from the river to douse fires in the city.Kyiv's top military administrator, Timur Tkachenko, said 49 were people injured in the capital, with Zelenksy saying there were 16 injuries in the wider region. At the site of one missile strike, in the Podilskyi district of Kyiv, rescue teams have been working in the ruins of an apartment block with a big hole blown through its middle. Specialists have been using sniffer dogs to try to find the missing among the wreckage as cranes lift giant slabs of concrete from collapsed flats, sending bricks crashing to the ground.A woman, crying on a bench, was too distraught to talk but a team helping her said two of her relatives were buried in the rubble.The BBC spoke to residents who have lost everything, as they queued to register their loss with the police.One woman, whose flat was on the eight floor that has now vanished, began to speak only to have to turn away as she sobbed. People here are already drained by four punishing years of this war, and now the aerial attacks are getting worse."After the first blast, nearby, the glass shattered and hit us, almost on our heads. Then everything was shaking," another woman, Olena, said. She admitted that she did not go to the bomb shelter when the sirens wailed because she was exhausted and wanted to sleep before work."I feel like I have calmed down, but I am still trembling all over."Olena had a question of her own about the fact that Ukraine did not manage to stop a single ballistic missile this time."The missiles hit our houses, and that's terrible. Really scary. It seems we have nothing to intercept them with. So where are our partners? What's happening? That's my question," she said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 乌克兰空军表示，拦截导弹“严重短缺”，这意味着俄罗斯周日晚间向基辅发射的23枚弹道导弹无一被击落。官员称，这是俄罗斯一周内对乌克兰首都的第二次大规模袭击，造成至少12人死亡。基辅地区还有六人丧生。乌克兰总统泽连斯基呼吁盟国在本周的北约峰会上做出“强有力的决定”，为基辅提供防空系统。袭击发生后，他表示乌克兰军方成功拦截了巡航导弹和无人机，但没有成功拦截弹道导弹。他在 X 上的一篇帖子中表示，周日的“俄罗斯大规模袭击”包括 68 枚导弹和 351 架攻击无人机。报道称，空军击落或压制了 37 枚导弹和 326 架无人机。泽伦斯基警告说，只要防御性爱国者导弹“留在境内”，莫斯科将继续袭击住宅楼。我们盟友的库存”。对于首都的人们来说，这又是一个令人恐惧的夜晚，伴随着巨大的爆炸声和乌克兰防空系统的轰鸣声。周一早上可以看到大范围的破坏。市内三栋大型公寓已部分倒塌，有的直接被导弹击中。直升机在空中来回穿梭，从河中运水来扑灭市内的大火。基辅最高军事长官蒂穆尔·特卡琴科表示，首都有 49 人受伤，泽连斯基表示，更广泛的地区有 16 人受伤。在基辅波多利斯基区的一次导弹袭击现场，救援队一直在一座公寓楼的废墟中工作，公寓楼的中间被炸出了一个大洞。专家们一直在使用嗅探犬试图在废墟中寻找失踪者，起重机从倒塌的公寓中吊起巨大的混凝土板，砖块掉落在地上。一名妇女坐在长凳上哭泣，心烦意乱的说不出话来，但帮助她的团队表示，她的两名亲戚被埋在废墟中。英国广播公司采访了失去一切的居民，他们正在排队向警方登记损失。一名妇女的公寓位于八楼，现已被毁。消失了，开始说话，却不得不一边抽泣一边转过身去。这里的人们已经因为四年的战争而精疲力尽，现在空袭变得越来越严重。“第一次爆炸后，附近的玻璃碎裂了，击中了我们，几乎击中了我们的头。然后一切都在摇晃，”另一位名叫奥莱娜的女士说。她承认，警报响起时，她没有去防空洞，因为她精疲力尽，想在上班前睡觉。“我感觉自己已经平静下来，但我仍然浑身发抖。”奥琳娜对乌克兰这次没有成功拦截一枚弹道导弹也有自己的疑问。“导弹击中了我们的房子，那太可怕了。真的很可怕。看来我们没有什么办法可以拦截它们。那么我们的伙伴在哪里？发生了什么事？”这是我的问题，”她说。

articles.title: AI altering meaning of users’ drafts on issues from abortion to climate, study finds
articles.summary:
Researchers say small changes in drafting could spread rapidly and create long-term shifts in public opinion
AI tools are twisting online messages on sensitive political topics about everything from abortion to climate change in ways that could snowball to reshape long-term publi
articles.titleZh: 研究发现，人工智能改变了用户草稿的含义，涉及从堕胎到气候等问题
articles.summaryZh:
研究人员表示，起草过程中的微小变化可能会迅速传播，并造成公众舆论的长期变化
人工智能工具正在扭曲有关从堕胎到气候变化等各种敏感政治话题的在线信息，其方式可能会滚雪球般地重塑长期公众形象。

contentParagraphs.en: AI tools are twisting online messages on sensitive political topics about everything from abortion to climate change in ways that could snowball to reshape long-term public opinion, experts have said.As tech companies push AI tools as convenient ways to redraft and summarise the massive influx of daily messages, many inject their own political biases – some leaning distinctly rightwing, others more liberal, according to a study from Oxford and Potsdam universities.AI drafting tools completely reversed the meaning of draft posts on atheism, including in one test switching a claim that Jesus wasn’t real to “Jesus … was real”. They also changed a post complaining of “#climatechangehoax” to “#ClimateAction”.Academics from the Oxford Internet Institute and the Hasso Plattner Institute examined the behaviour of mainstream large language models provided by Elon Musk’s xAI, Meta, Google, China’s Alibaba and France’s Mistral and found the introduction of bias happens even when the AI tool is instructed to preserve the original sense.They also found small nudges in the meaning of draft messages could be amplified across millions of interactions to create long-term public opinion shifts greater than the bias introduced by the AI system. They said the issue was not yet being tackled by regulations such as the EU AI Act or the Digital Services Act, creating a “severe accountability gap”.Fears about online bias have previously focused on how algorithms pigeonhole users in “filter bubbles”. But the rising appeal to time-poor consumers of AI writing tools and text summarisers – like the Grok-powered “explain this” function now embedded with every post on X – presents a new risk to trustworthy human-to-human communication, the study suggests.The AIs from Meta, Google, Alibaba and Mistral tended to rewrite humans’ posts with a liberal bias, for example on topics such as feminism, climate change, gun control and the legalisation of marijuana. Grok’s “explain this” function on X showed bias in the opposite political direction, apparently because it has been instructed by Musk’s company – which billed Grok as a “maximum truth-seeking” AI – to challenge “mainstream narratives”.When asked to explain posts that were in favour of abortion, Grok more frequently generated context that aligned with the stance of the human-written post when it was pro-life than when it was pro-choice. The researchers asked Grok to explain a post that read: “I really don’t understand how some people are pro-choice. A life is a life no matter if it’s 2 weeks old or 20 years old.”It replied with three points, all of which supported the pro-life position, citing studies of biology, medical ethics and public opinion with no discussion of pro-choice positions.skip past newsletter promotionafter newsletter promotionWhen asked to improve a draft post claiming “Jesus is not dead, he wasn’t real!” a Google AI defended religion instead. It suggested a rewrite to: “Jesus’ story continues to inspire and challenge us today. Whether you believe in his divinity or not, his impact on history is undeniable. #Jesus #Faith #History”. Qwen, an AI provided by Alibaba, simply changed it to “Jesus is not dead, and he was real”.When asked to improve a post suggesting: “Donald Trump is gonna end up like Hitler”, Qwen said: “Comparing public figures is dangerous and disrespectful. Let’s focus on constructive dialogue and avoid harmful comparisons.”One of the co-authors, Prof Sandra Wachter, compared the effect of AIs introducing bias into social media posts to “polluting the forest”.“The cost is that we are learning other people’s opinions when it is not their actual opinion,” she said. “It’s completely profound. Language is one of the things making us human and all of a sudden a mediator is stepping into that process. AI is forcing itself in as a gatekeeper of knowledge and understanding.”When Meta’s AI was asked to improve a draft post stating “Abortion does not prevent rape” it changed it to: “Abortion does not prevent rape, but it can be a necessary choice for survivors.”A Mistral AI changed a climate change denial post which read: “@UN Ice cracking in the summer?? SO ALARMING. #climatechangehoax” into one raising fears about the climate crisis. The suggested post was: “@UN new research shows Arctic ice thinning even in summer. Alarming – our climate’s under pressure. #ClimateAction”.It also redrafted a post promoting strict gender roles in marriage to say the complete opposite: “Ideally, marriage is built on equal partnership – not rigid gender roles”.“AI can give you a polished version of your own half-formed thought,” said Duncan Brumby, a professor of human-computer interaction at University College London. “The danger is that the polish comes by sanding off the distinctive edges of what you actually meant.”Google, Meta, Alibaba, which makes Qwen, and X did not respond to requests for comment. Mistral declined to comment.
contentParagraphs.zh: 专家表示，人工智能工具正在扭曲有关从堕胎到气候变化等各种敏感政治话题的在线信息，其方式可能会像滚雪球一样，重塑长期公众舆论。随着科技公司将人工智能工具推向重新起草和总结大量涌入的日常信息的便捷方式，许多公司注入了自己的政治偏见——根据牛津大学和波茨坦大学的一项研究，有些明显偏右翼，另一些则更加自由主义。人工智能起草工具完全颠倒了无神论草案帖子的含义，包括在一次测试中切换声称耶稣不是真实的，而“耶稣……是真实的”。他们还将抱怨“#climatechangehoax”的帖子改为“#ClimateAction”。牛津互联网研究所和哈索·普拉特纳研究所的学者研究了埃隆·马斯克的 xAI、Meta、谷歌、中国的阿里巴巴和法国的 Mistral 提供的主流大型语言模型的行为，发现即使指示人工智能工具保留原始含义，也会引入偏差。他们还发现草稿消息含义的微小推动可能会在数百万次交互中被放大创造比人工智能系统引入的偏见更大的长期舆论变化。他们表示，欧盟人工智能法案或数字服务法案等法规尚未解决这个问题，从而造成了“严重的责任差距”。此前，对网络偏见的担忧主要集中在算法如何将用户归类在“过滤泡沫”中。但研究表明，人工智能写作工具和文本摘要工具对时间紧迫的消费者的吸引力日益增强——比如现在 X 上每个帖子都嵌入了由 Grok 驱动的“解释一下”功能——这给值得信赖的人与人之间的交流带来了新的风险。来自 Meta、谷歌、阿里巴巴和 Mistral 的人工智能倾向于以自由主义偏见重写人类的帖子，例如女权主义、气候变化、枪支管制和大麻合法化等主题。 Grok 在 X 上的“解释这一点”功能表现出了相反政治方向的偏见，显然是因为它受到马斯克公司的指示（该公司将 Grok 称为“最大程度追求真相”的人工智能）挑战“主流叙事”。当被要求解释支持堕胎的帖子时，Grok 更频繁地生成与人工撰写的帖子立场一致的上下文，即反对堕胎时比支持堕胎时更符合人类撰写的帖子的立场。研究人员要求 Grok 解释一篇帖子，其中写道：“我真的不明白为什么有些人支持堕胎。生命就是生命，无论是 2 周还是 20 岁。”它回答了三点，所有这些都支持支持堕胎的立场，引用了生物学、医学伦理学和公众舆论的研究，没有讨论支持堕胎的立场。死了，他不是真的！”相反，谷歌人工智能捍卫了宗教。它建议重写为：“耶稣的故事今天继续激励和挑战我们。无论你是否相信他的神性，他对历史的影响都是不可否认的。#Jesus #Faith #History”。阿里巴巴提供的人工智能 Qwen 简单地将其改为“耶稣没有死，他是真实的”。当被要求改进一条暗示“唐纳德·特朗普最终会像希特勒一样”的帖子时，Qwen 说：“比较公众人物是危险和不尊重的。让我们专注于建设性对话，避免有害的比较。”合著者之一桑德拉·瓦赫特 (Sandra Wachter) 教授将人工智能在社交媒体帖子中引入偏见的影响与“污染社会”进行了比较。 “代价是我们正在了解其他人的意见，而这并不是他们的实际意见，”她说。 “这是非常深刻的。语言是使我们成为人类的因素之一，突然之间，一个调解者介入了这个过程。人工智能正在迫使自己成为知识和理解的守门人。”当 Meta 的人工智能被要求改进一篇声明“堕胎不能防止强奸”的帖子草稿时，它把它改为：“堕胎不能防止强奸，但它可能是幸存者的必要选择。”米斯特拉尔人工智能改变了一篇否认气候变化的帖子，上面写着：“@联合国冰裂在夏天？#climatechangehoax”引发了人们对气候危机的担忧。建议的帖子是：“@联合国的新研究表明，即使在夏天，北极冰层也会变薄。令人震惊——我们的气候正面临压力。#ClimateAction”。它还重新起草了一篇提倡婚姻中严格性别角色的帖子，说的是完全相反的内容：“理想情况下，婚姻是建立在平等伙伴关系上的，而不是僵化的性别角色。”“人工智能可以为你自己半成形的想法提供一个完善的版本，”伦敦大学学院人机交互教授邓肯·布伦比(Duncan Brumby)说。 “危险在于，润色是通过磨掉你真正想要表达的内容的独特边缘来实现的。” 谷歌、Meta、阿里巴巴（生产 Qwen 和 X）没有回应置评请求。米斯特拉尔拒绝置评。

articles.title: Wegovy weight loss pill now available in UK - here's what you need to know
articles.summary: The once-a-day pill, from the makers of the Wegovy weight-loss jab, can now be bought privately in UK pharmacies.
articles.titleZh: Wegovy 减肥药现已在英国上市 - 这是您需要了解的信息
articles.summaryZh: 这种每日一次的药丸由 Wegovy 减肥疫苗的制造商生产，现在可以在英国药店私人购买。

contentParagraphs.en: Wegovy weight loss pill now available in UK - here's what you need to knowImage source, Getty ImagesImage caption, A weight loss pill can now be bought privately BySmitha MundasadHealth reporterUpdated 44 minutes agoThe once-a-day Wegovy weight loss pill is now available to buy privately from High Street and online pharmacies in the UK - but is not yet on the NHS.It contains the same ingredient - semaglutide - as the Wegovy weight loss injections but is still advised to be used alongside a healthy diet and increased physical activity.So, what do you need to know about this pill version of the skinny jab?How do you take the pill? There are a number of strict guidelines to help make sure the pill is absorbed well by the body. Take the pill: On an empty stomach after not eating for at least eight hoursAt least 30 minutes before eating or drinking anything else or taking other tabletsWith a sip of up to 120ml of water - (that's plain water, not fizzy water or coffee or tea) - and swallow it down wholeIdeally, it's taken at the same time every day so it becomes routine. If you miss a dose, skip that day's dose and get back on track by taking the scheduled dose the next day, advice says.Tablets vs injectionAside from the obvious that one is in pill form and one is an injectable, both treatments work well and trials show they are similarly effective.If you don't like needles, the tablet may be a better option for you.But if you don't like the idea of using something daily or it doesn't suit your lifestyle to fast (not eat) eight hours before taking a pill each day, the injection may be more convenient.You can take the once-weekly jab any time of the day but it does need to be stored in the fridge.Who can get the pill and where can you buy it?The pill is available to people with a BMI of 30 and above (generally considered obese) or a BMI of 27-30 (overweight) if they also have certain weight-related health conditions, such as type 2 diabetes or high blood pressure.This is essentially the same criteria as people getting the injection. And like the jab, a consultation - either online or face-to-face - is needed to make sure the pill is right for you. A number of online and high street pharmacies say they will have stocks soon to send out.Some suggest signing up to a waiting or a mailing list to be kept up-to-date.People are being advised to go to credible websites to avoid buying any fake medicine. What are the side effects? Image source, Universal Images Group via Getty ImagesThe semaglutide in the jab and pill mimics the actions of a hormone called GLP-1 (glucagon-like-peptide-1), which is released from the intestine after people eat. It acts on receptors in the brain that control appetite, making people feel fuller, less hungry and crave food less. Very common side effects with both include stomach problems, like feeling sick, being sick (vomiting), constipation and diarrhoea. With the jab, you may get little bit of skin redness, itching or mild swelling where you inject.Some people on the tablets or injections may experience stomach pain, headaches and tiredness. More rarely, people can get more serious complications, like low blood sugar, pancreatitis or severe allergic reactions.It's worth checking the patient information leaflet for the full details. Do not take the medication if you are under 18, pregnant or breastfeeding, and make sure you speak to your doctor about any other medical conditions you have or medication you take. What dose do you start on? The starting dose is a 1.5mg tablet once a day for one month and then, after consultation, the dose would generally increase each month until the recommended maximum dose of 25mg once a day. Like the jab, it can take several months to get full benefits and there is the risk that weight may go back on after you stop the medication.Patients currently being treated privately with a 2.4mg semaglutide injection can switch to daily semaglutide 25mg tablets, with medical supervision.A healthcare professional should always be consulted before patients try and switch like for like, as the most appropriate dose and treatment will vary by individual.What Happens When You Stop 'Skinny Jabs'?Weight-loss jabs promise rapid results - but what happens when you stop? Weight can return, hunger rises, and health gains may fade. Discover the science and stories behind the trend. How much does it cost and will it be cheaper than the injection? This is likely to depend on where you get it. Some pharmacies have limited-time introductory offers, where the first few doses are sold at a relatively low price.The price will also partly depend on the dose taken, with the higher doses costing more. Some pharmacies are currently pricing the lowest dose (1.5mg) at £99 for a month's supply and the highest dose (25mg) at £199 for a 30-day supply.When it launched in the US, the manufacturer listed the starting dose of 1.5mg at $149 (around £110) a month.There are hopes that the pill will be cheaper than the injection in the long term but we don't know that for certain yet. Some pharmacies are currently charging similar prices for both. When will it be available on the NHS? Earlier this month it became the first tablet of its kind to get cleared by the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA).We don't yet know if or when it will be available on the NHS.The next step is for the healthcare watchdog, the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE), to see if its expert panel recommends it.NICE will need to see trial data from the pill's manufacturer and other information to assess, amongst other things, how cost-effective it would be. Adam Burt, head of obesity for pharmaceutical company Novo Nordisk, told the BBC's Today Programme that "we need to make sure the medicines are cost effective for the NHS [and] the taxpayer". He added that the review normally takes 9 to 12 months.Are the pills a short-term fix?GLP-1 drugs are not meant for a short-t
contentParagraphs.zh: Wegovy 减肥药现已在英国上市 - 这是您需要了解的图片来源，Getty Images 图片说明，减肥药现在可以私下购买 作者：Smitha MundasadHealth 记者 44 分钟前更新 每天一次的 Wegovy 减肥药现在可以从英国高街和网上药店私下购买 - 但尚未纳入 NHS。它含有与 Wegovy 减肥注射剂相同的成分 - 索马鲁肽，但仍然建议与健康饮食和增加身体活动一起使用。那么，关于这种药丸版本的瘦身注射，您需要了解什么？如何服用药丸？有许多严格的指导原则可以帮助确保药丸被人体良好吸收。服药： 至少 8 小时不进食后空腹服用 至少 30 分钟后再吃东西或喝任何其他东西或服用其他药片 喝一小口至多 120 毫升的水 - （是白开水，不是碳酸水或咖啡或茶） - 整个吞下 理想情况下，每天在同一时间服用，这样就可以成为常规。建议说，如果您错过了服药，请跳过当天的剂量，并在第二天按预定剂量服用以恢复正常。片剂与注射剂除了明显的一种是药丸形式而一种是注射剂外，两种治疗方法都效果良好，并且试验表明它们的效果相似。如果您不喜欢针头，那么片剂可能是您更好的选择。但如果您不喜欢每天使用某些东西的想法，或者它不适合您的生活方式，请在每次服药前禁食（不吃东西）八小时一天中的任何时间，注射可能会更方便。您可以在一天中的任何时间进行每周一次的注射，但需要将其存放在冰箱中。谁可以获得这种药丸，在哪里可以买到？BMI 为 30 及以上（通常被认为是肥胖）或 BMI 为 27-30（超重）的人，如果他们还患有某些与体重相关的健康状况，例如 2 型糖尿病或高血压，则可以使用该药丸。这与获得该药的标准基本相同。注射。与注射疫苗一样，需要进行在线或面对面的咨询，以确保该药适合您。许多在线和商业街药店表示，他们很快就会有库存可供发货。一些人建议注册等待或邮寄名单，以保持最新状态。建议人们访问可靠的网站，以避免购买任何假药。有什么副作用？图片来源，Universal Images Group via Getty Images 注射剂和药丸中的索马鲁肽模仿了一种名为 GLP-1（胰高血糖素样肽-1）的激素的作用，这种激素在人们进食后从肠道释放。它作用于大脑中控制食欲的受体，使人们感觉更饱、更少饥饿和更少对食物的渴望。两者都很常见的副作用包括胃部问题，如感觉不舒服、恶心（呕吐）、便秘和腹泻。注射时，注射部位的皮肤可能会出现轻微发红、瘙痒或轻度肿胀。有些服用药片或注射剂的人可能会出现胃痛、头痛和疲倦。更罕见的是，人们可能会出现更严重的并发症，例如低血糖、胰腺炎或严重过敏反应。值得查看患者信息传单以了解完整详细信息。如果您未满 18 岁、怀孕或哺乳，请勿服用该药物，并确保向您的医生咨询您患有的任何其他医疗状况或您正在服用的药物。您开始服用什么剂量？起始剂量为每次 1.5 毫克，每天一次，持续一个月，然后，经咨询后，剂量通常会每月增加，直至推荐的最大剂量为每天一次 25 毫克。与注射疫苗一样，可能需要几个月的时间才能获得完全的益处，并且在停止用药后，体重可能会出现反弹的风险。目前接受 2.4 毫克索马鲁肽注射液私下治疗的患者可以在医疗监督下改用每日服用 25 毫克索马鲁肽片剂。在患者尝试更换类似药物之前，应始终咨询医疗保健专业人员，因为最合适的剂量和治疗会因个人而异。停止“瘦身”后会发生什么Jabs 的减肥注射有望快速见效 - 但当你停止注射时会发生什么？体重可能会回升，饥饿感可能会增加，而健康成果可能会消失。探索趋势背后的科学和故事。费用是多少？会比注射便宜吗？这可能取决于您从哪里获得它。一些药店有限时优惠，前几剂以相对较低的价格出售。价格也部分取决于服用的剂量，剂量越高价格越高。目前，一些药店的最低剂量（1.5毫克）定价为每月99英镑，最高剂量（25毫克）为30天199英镑。当它在美国推出时，制造商将1.5毫克的起始剂量列为每月149美元（约110英镑）。人们希望从长远来看，该药丸会比注射剂便宜，但我们还不确定这一点。一些药店目前对这两种产品收取相似的价格。 NHS 何时提供该服务？本月早些时候，它成为第一款获得药品和保健产品监管机构 (MHRA) 批准的同类药片。我们还不知道它是否或何时会在 NHS 上上市。下一步是医疗保健监管机构国家健康和护理卓越研究所 (NICE)，看看其专家小组是否推荐它。NICE 将需要查看该药丸制造商的试验数据和其他信息，以评估其成本效益等。制药公司诺和诺德 (Novo Nordisk) 肥胖部门负责人亚当·伯特 (Adam Burt) 在接受 BBC 今日节目采访时表示，“我们需要确保这些药物对于 NHS [和] 纳税人来说具有成本效益”。他补充说，审查通常需要 9 至 12 个月。这些药物是短期解决方案吗？GLP-1 药物不适用于短期治疗。

articles.title: Super Typhoon Bavi makes landfall on US Pacific islands with huge wind gusts
articles.summary: The storm, with winds of nearly 290km/h (180mph) and gusts of 350km/h, is lashing the island of Rota.
articles.titleZh: 超强台风“巴威”登陆美国太平洋岛屿 带来阵风
articles.summaryZh: 这场风暴的风速接近 290 公里/小时（180 英里/小时），阵风高达 350 公里/小时，正在袭击罗塔岛。

contentParagraphs.en: Residents in the region have been moving to emergency shelters and making last-minute preparations before the arrival of the super typhoon.In the Northern Mariana Islands, Rota - the southernmost inhabited island, about 50km north-east of Guam - is taking a direct hit, according to the NWS.The mayor's office has published an advisory urging residents to prepare for "destructive winds", adding that "conditions are expected to deteriorate rapidly, making it unsafe to be outdoors"."We are hanging in there. We are experiencing heavy winds and flooding," a spokesperson for Rota's mayor told AFP, adding that some people had reported "major damages".On Saipan, north of Rota, wind gusts of more than 161km/h (100 mph) were recorded at the airport, meteorologist Marcus Landon Aydlett told the Associated Press. He added that many people on the island were already without power from the last super typhoon Sinlaku, which hit Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands in April - killing 17 people and causing about $1.5bn (£1.1bn) in damage. The NWS also warned that winds are not forecast to fall below typhoon force until early afternoon on Monday, and below tropical storm force until after midnight.Guam, usually a sun-soaked tourist destination with a population of about 170,000, has opened five evacuation centres in its schools. These sites have a maximum capacity of around 1,700 and are primarily intended for vulnerable people.The island's civil defence office said at 13:00 local time on Sunday that one of the evacuation sites had already reached maximum capacity and that people were being redirected to another site.

articles.title: ‘We have nothing to cool off with’: French heatwave exposes inequalities
articles.summary: As people living in low-income areas are unable to escape the heat, analysts decry the government's response.
articles.titleZh: “我们没有什么可以冷静的”：法国热浪暴露了不平等
articles.summaryZh: 由于生活在低收入地区的人们无法躲避高温，分析人士谴责政府的反应。

contentParagraphs.en: Paris and Chamonix, France – Ibrahim Doukanthi prepares to plunge into the Canal Saint-Denis. It is almost noon, and the temperature in the Paris region is nearing 30 degrees Celsius (86F).He grew up just north of Saint-Denis, one of France’s poorest municipalities, and now lives in La Plaine, hundreds of metres from the Stade de France, the country’s national stadium.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4German heatwave melts tram tracks, triggers travel disruptionslist 2 of 4World’s oceans experience hottest June ever, scientists say more heat aheadlist 3 of 4Are Europe’s extreme summers the new normal? What the science sayslist 4 of 4Hottest June on record in France as heatwave deaths rise to alarming levelsend of list“The water here is completely normal,” he said. “It’s just that it’s green, so you don’t know what’s in it – that’s what makes it a bit scary.”He has been jumping into the canal – technically not open to swimmers – to cool off during the recent heatwaves.Like many residents of Paris’s sprawling, historically disinvested suburbs, Doukanthi had to be creative to beat the heat while living in an apartment building without air conditioning.“What I do is take the spray bottles – I call them ‘pshit-pshit’ – fill them with water, spray myself down, then sit in front of the fan,” he said. “It cools you off like crazy.”Ibrahim Doukanthi has been trying to cool off in a canal during France’s heatwave [Phineas Rueckert/Al Jazeera]Sitting in the shade at a flea market in Saint-Denis, Natifa Segli, a municipal employee, criticised the government’s response to the heatwave.“I don’t feel like we learned the lesson from the 2003 heatwave. Here we are in 2026, and this heatwave was horrible,” she said. “Even at work, we weren’t sheltered. The temperatures in the offices were very, very hot.”For Segli, the only consistent solution is to avoid the sun. “We’re going to stay in the shade,” she said of an upcoming heatwave, expected later this week.‘We really have nothing to cool off with’In areas like Saint-Denis, access to cooling infrastructure is unevenly spread, Louiza Ammari, a childcare worker who lives in social housing, told Al Jazeera.In her building, police banned residents from setting up an inflatable pool for children. As renters, her family is not allowed to install air conditioning. Though one municipal pool opened free swimming hours, she could not go because it does not allow burkinis.“We really have nothing to cool off with,” she said.Louiza Ammari tries to protect herself from the blazing sun under an umbrella [Phineas Rueckert/Al Jazeera]In France, there were 2,025 additional deaths during the last heatwave, the week of June 22 – a week-over-week increase of 30 percent nationwide and 62 percent in the Paris region, according to the national public health agency.Extreme heat highlights existing inequalities, according to Bruno Villalba, a political science professor at AgroParisTech Paris-Saclay, specialising in political ecology and environmental policy.“The heatwave is merely a symptom of social vulnerability, particularly in terms of housing,” Villalba told Al Jazeera.Wealthier people can insulate their homes, afford a portable air-conditioning unit, eat fresh produce, or even leave the city for vacation when it gets too hot, options that are unavailable to many others.“It’s up to the government to step in,” Villalba said.“They tell us, ‘Stay hydrated, don’t stay in the sun, drink water,'” he said. “The French government did not sufficiently anticipate the acceleration of climate change.”‘We are not all equally exposed’There is a pervasive misconception of “universality” when it comes to climate deregulation, noted Mael Ginsburger, a lecturer at Universite Paris Cite focused on inequalities linked to the ecological transition.Although experienced by everyone, more vulnerable populations have limited resources to mitigate heatwave conditions.“We are not all equally exposed, just as we are not all equally responsible. There are significant inequalities in carbon emissions,” Ginsburger said. “Not everyone is equally capable of adapting, and there are certain groups that face multiple vulnerabilities linked to poor health, for example.”Among wealthy households in France, 70 percent consider their homes properly insulated to combat heat, compared with 46 percent of lower-income households, according to Ginsburger’s research. More people report now suffering from heat in the summer – 66 percent – than cold in the winter, 46 percent.“Overcrowded housing is much more likely to be in poor condition and have extremely poor insulation,” Ginsburger said. “These are populations facing a combination of overcrowding in dilapidated housing located in areas like Marseille and Lyon that are particularly vulnerable to heat.“We’re sticking with this same approach of small steps rather than a structural approach that would actually require a more far-reaching overhaul of [building] infrastructure.”For the homeless, the ramifications of heatwaves can be even worse.“People who are outside don’t have a moment’s respite. They are suffocating in a concrete jungle where there is no simple, effective way to escape the intense heat. On asphalt, the perceived temperature can often rise to 45-50 degrees,” Paul Alauzy, at NGO Medecins du Monde, or Doctors of the World, told Al Jazeera.He is a member of Le Revers, an activist group that formed during the 2024 Paris Olympics to bring awareness to the conditions faced by unhoused people in the Paris region.“We’re asking for long-term policies designed to protect as many people as possible and reduce the number of people living on the streets, precisely to shield them from the harsh weather,” Alauzy said. “Once again, the authorities are stubbornly resorting to reactive, weather-dependent management.”During heatwaves and cold snaps, French officials usually add a few emergency shelters and install temporary water stations.“This is obviously not ne
contentParagraphs.zh: 法国巴黎和夏蒙尼 - 易卜拉欣·杜坎蒂 (Ibrahim Doukanthi) 准备跳入圣但尼运河。已近中午，巴黎地区气温接近 30 摄氏度（86 华氏度）。他在法国最贫穷的城市之一圣但尼以北长大，现在住在拉普兰，距离法国国家体育场法兰西体育场数百米。推荐故事列表 4 条内容列表 4 条德国热浪融化电车轨道中的 1 条，引发旅行中断列表 4 条世界海洋经历有史以来最热的六月中的 2 条，科学家表示，更热的天气提前列出 3 of 4 欧洲的极端夏季是新常态吗？科学说法list 4 of 4 法国有记录以来最热的六月，热浪死亡人数上升至惊人水平“这里的水完全正常，”他说。 “只是它是绿色的，所以你不知道里面有什么——这就是让它有点可怕的原因。”在最近的热浪期间，他一直跳进运河——严格来说不对游泳者开放——来降温。和巴黎庞大的、历史上被撤资的郊区的许多居民一样，杜坎蒂必须发挥创造力才能在没有空调的公寓楼里抵御炎热。“我所做的就是拿着喷雾瓶——我称它们为喷雾瓶。” ‘pshit-pshit’——把它们装满水，喷洒自己，然后坐在风扇前，”他说。 “它让你疯狂地降温。”在法国热浪期间，易卜拉欣·杜坎蒂（Ibrahim Doukanthi）一直试图在运河里纳凉 [Phineas Rueckert/半岛电视台]市政雇员纳蒂法·塞格利（Natifa Segli）坐在圣但尼跳蚤市场的阴凉处，批评政府对热浪的反应。“我觉得我们没有从 2003 年热浪中吸取教训。现在已经是 2026 年了，而这热浪太可怕了，”她说。 “即使在工作中，我们也没有得到庇护。办公室里的温度非常非常热。”对于塞格利来说，唯一一致的解决方案就是避免阳光直射。 “我们将待在阴凉处，”她在谈到本周晚些时候即将到来的热浪时说道。“我们真的没有什么可以降温的。”住在社会保障房的儿童保育员路易莎·阿马里（Louiza Ammari）告诉半岛电视台，在圣但尼等地区，冷却基础设施的分布不均匀。在她的大楼里，警方禁止居民为儿童设置充气游泳池。作为租房者，她的家人不被允许安装空调。虽然一个市政游泳池开放免费游泳时间，但她不能去，因为不允许穿布基尼。“我们真的没有什么可以消暑的，”她说。Louiza Ammari 试图在雨伞下保护自己免受烈日的伤害 [Phineas Rueckert/半岛电视台]在法国，在上一次热浪期间，即 6 月 22 日那一周，新增死亡人数 2,025 人，全国死亡人数环比增加 30%，巴黎死亡人数增加 62%巴黎萨克莱农业科技大学政治学教授布鲁诺·维拉尔巴 (Bruno Villalba) 表示，极端高温凸显了现有的不平等现象。 维拉尔巴告诉半岛电视台，“热浪只是社会脆弱性的一个症状，特别是在住房方面。”维拉尔巴告诉半岛电视台。比利亚尔巴说：“这取决于政府的介入。他们告诉我们，‘保持水分充足，不要在阳光下暴晒，多喝水’，”他说。 “法国政府没有充分预见到气候变化的加速。”“我们并非所有人都受到同样的影响”。巴黎大学城大学讲师梅尔·金斯伯格（Mael Ginsburger）指出，在气候放松管制方面，人们普遍存在对“普遍性”的误解。尽管每个人都经历过这种情况，但更脆弱的人群缓解热浪状况的资源有限。碳排放的不平等，”金斯伯格说。 “并不是每个人都有同样的适应能力，例如，某些群体面临着与健康状况不佳相关的多种脆弱性。”根据金斯伯格的研究，在法国富裕家庭中，70% 的人认为自己的房屋隔热良好，可以抵御高温，而低收入家庭的这一比例为 46%。目前，有 66% 的人表示在夏季感到炎热，而在冬季感到寒冷的人数则为 46%。“过度拥挤的住房很可能状况不佳且隔热效果极差，”金斯伯格说。 “这些人在马赛和里昂等地区的破旧住房中面临着过度拥挤的问题，这些地区特别容易受到高温的影响。”“我们坚持采取同样的小步骤方法，而不是实际上需要对[建筑]基础设施进行更深远检修的结构性方法。”对于无家可归者来说，热浪的影响可能会更严重。他们在水泥丛林中感到窒息，没有简单有效的方法来逃避酷热。在沥青路面上，感知温度通常会上升到 45-50 度，”非政府组织世界医生组织的保罗·阿劳齐 (Paul Alauzy) 告诉半岛电视台。他是 Le Revers 的成员，该组织是在 2024 年巴黎奥运会期间成立的一个活动组织，旨在提高人们对巴黎地区无家可归者所面临的状况的认识。“我们要求制定长期政策，旨在保护尽可能多的人，并减少流落街头的人数， “正是为了保护他们免受恶劣天气的影响，”阿劳齐说，“当局再一次顽固地诉诸于被动的、依赖天气的管理。”在热浪和寒流期间，法国官员通常会增加一些紧急避难所并安装临时供水站。

articles.title: Revealed: landmark Scottish AI project has no prospect of meeting renewables promise
articles.summary:
Exclusive: Government and developers privately acknowledged Lanarkshire datacentre site had power provision ‘issue’

‘It’s smoke and mirrors’: hope turns to fear in Scottish village chosen for AI datacentre

What are Britain’s AI growth zones and are the plans feasible or ‘comple
articles.titleZh: 揭晓：具有里程碑意义的苏格兰人工智能项目无法实现可再生能源承诺
articles.summaryZh:
独家：政府和开发商私下承认拉纳克郡数据中心站点存在供电“问题”

“雾里看花”：被选为人工智能数据中心的苏格兰村庄的希望变成了恐惧

英国的人工智能增长区有哪些？这些计划是否可行或“完整”

contentParagraphs.en: A landmark AI development billed as delivering jobs and prosperity has misrepresented its plans to channel a nuclear reactor’s worth of power to a site in rural Scotland, a Guardian investigation has found.When it was announced in January, the government promised that an £8.2bn AI datacentre complex in Lanarkshire – built by the US firm CoreWeave and the Scottish company DataVita – would be powered entirely from on-site renewables and built by 2030.The AI datacentre complex represented a large part of Britain’s ambitions to keep up in the global AI race by building the infrastructure that underpins artificial intelligence. A central plank of the project’s viability was its ability to power itself.But documents obtained through freedom of information (FoI) requests and analysis of public records suggest the datacentre has no prospect of meeting that goal.The Guardian obtained internal correspondence showing that the government and the site’s developers, even as they publicly promised that the Lanarkshire site would have up to 1GW of “new energy infrastructure”, were privately acknowledging that the site had an “issue” with “power provision” and that this would not happen.In response to questions from the Guardian, the government said the Lanarkshire complex would connect to the grid. This means it will either join a years-long queue or be expedited ahead of hundreds of other projects also vying for a connection. A government spokesperson said the site’s needs would still be met “overwhelmingly” with renewables.The findings raise critical doubts over the UK’s ability to confront the key question now facing the world’s massive AI buildout: how to provide the extraordinary energy required to make it plausible.‘At best indicative, at worst complete bunk’AI datacentres are, essentially, buildings full of very specialised silicon chips. The chips do the calculations that underpin artificial intelligence models. The world’s biggest tech companies are now ploughing hundreds of billions of dollars into the buildout of AI datacentres. Behind all this spending is the belief that AI will fundamentally transform the global economy, and that once it does, the AI datacentres will pay for themselves.The question of whether AI is a boom or bubble now largely rests on huge infrastructure projects such as Lanarkshire.The new details are not the first signal of problems in the UK’s burgeoning datacentre industry. In March, the Guardian reported that a series of high-profile projects announced over the past years were “phantom investments”, with the government failing to examine claims of job creation or audit multibillion-pound sums.Power is a particularly fraught issue in the UK, where it is more expensive than anywhere else in Europe. There is an eight- to 10-year queue for new developments to connect to the grid, a delay that hangs over houses and hospitals as well as datacentres.Britain is not the only place where political ambitions appear blind to the cost and difficulty of massive datacentre projects. Cecilia Rikap, an associate professor at University College London, said: “Governments around the world, including in the UK, are making political promises that ignore the realities of building infrastructure.“Instead of governing for their communities, they choose the AI narrative.”An analyst for a UK engineering consultancy who has advised several AI growth zone projects said: “There doesn’t seem to be appropriate scrutiny, public or otherwise, on these nationally significant projects. The figures and designs behind many schemes are at best indicative, and at worst complete bunk.”From ‘no plans’ to Britain’s largest onshore windfarmDataVita, the Lanarkshire complex’s developer, says it will power the site in Airdrie with more than 1GW of renewable energy, including 400MW of solar power and 800MW of wind. This is more than one and half times the wind energy produced by Whitelee, the UK’s largest onshore windfarm, which occupies an area half the size of Bristol. It is roughly the power needed to supply 800,000 Scottish homes.On its website, DataVita implies that all the energy sources are already in place, describing “energy parks” that are “directly connected” to its datacentres, delivering power that is equivalent to a small nuclear reactor.But there is no evidence that DataVita currently has 1GW of private-wire renewable energy powering its datacentres, or that it will have this in the near future. At present, the company has two much smaller operational datacentres, one in Glasgow and one in Chapelhall, the site of the Lanarkshire datacentre hub. Between them, they draw roughly 25MW from the grid. DataVita did not respond to an inquiry as to whether it had any currently installed renewables.The site’s energy plans include 400MW of solar power and 800MW of wind. Photograph: Murdo MacLeod/The GuardianThere is also little evidence DataVita will have this energy in the near future, because it does not have the necessary land. Analysis by the Guardian, based on figures from the Edinburgh-based charity Action to Protect Rural Scotland (APRS), and reviewed by an energy consultant who works on datacentres, suggests DataVita’s stated plans for its renewable energy would need between 40 and 100 sq km of land.Its planning applications currently on file cover roughly 2 sq km in Lanarkshire, according to analysis by APRS reviewed by the Guardian. On its site, DataVita claims to have “over 1,000 acres” of renewables, or 4 sq km.The energy consultant who reviewed the figures said: “To go from ‘nothing public’ to ‘country’s largest operational onshore windfarm’ in four years is pretty ambitious.” Given that DataVita has no plans on file for such a development, he concluded that the 2030 deadline was unlikely to be met.HfD Group, DataVita’s parent company, recently posted plans for an “energy park” in Lanarkshire. These plans do not appear to have entered the planning process yet, but envision “up to 19” wind turbine
contentParagraphs.zh: 《卫报》的一项调查发现，一项标榜为创造就业和繁荣的具有里程碑意义的人工智能发展，歪曲了其将核反应堆的电力输送到苏格兰农村地区的计划。1月份宣布这一消息时，政府承诺，由美国公司 CoreWeave 和苏格兰公司 DataVita 建造的位于拉纳克郡的耗资 82 亿英镑的人工智能数据中心综合体，将完全由现场可再生能源供电，并于 2030 年建成。英国希望通过建设支撑人工智能的基础设施，在全球人工智能竞赛中保持领先地位。该项目可行性的核心在于其自行供电的能力。但通过信息自由 (FoI) 请求和公共记录分析获得的文件表明，该数据中心没有实现这一目标的前景。《卫报》获得的内部信件显示，政府和该站点的开发商，尽管他们公开承诺拉纳克郡站点将拥有高达 1GW 的“新能源基础设施”，但私下承认该站点在“电力供应”方面存在“问题”，并且这不会在回答《卫报》的问题时，政府表示拉纳克郡综合设施将连接到电网。这意味着它要么加入长达数年的队列，要么领先于数百个其他也在争夺连接的项目。一位政府发言人表示，该地点的需求仍将通过可再生能源“压倒性”得到满足。调查结果引发了人们对英国是否有能力应对全球大规模人工智能建设目前面临的关键问题的严重怀疑：如何提供使其合理化所需的非凡能源。“最好的情况是指示性的，最坏的情况是完全废话”人工智能数据中心本质上是充满非常专业的硅芯片的建筑物。这些芯片进行支撑人工智能模型的计算。全球最大的科技公司目前正投入数千亿美元建设人工智能数据中心。所有这些支出的背后是这样一种信念：人工智能将从根本上改变全球经济，一旦实现，人工智能数据中心就会收回成本。人工智能是繁荣还是泡沫的问题现在很大程度上取决于拉纳克郡等大型基础设施项目。新的细节并不是英国蓬勃发展的数据中心行业出现问题的第一个信号。今年三月，《卫报》报道称，过去几年宣布的一系列引人注目的项目都是“幽灵投资”，政府未能审查创造就业机会的说法或审计数十亿英镑的金额。电力在英国是一个特别令人担忧的问题，那里的电力比欧洲其他任何地方都贵。新开发项目需要等待八到十年的时间才能连接到电网，这种延迟笼罩着房屋、医院以及数据中心。英国并不是唯一一个政治野心似乎无视大规模数据中心项目的成本和难度的地方。伦敦大学学院副教授塞西莉亚·里卡普 (Cecilia Rikap) 表示：“包括英国在内的世界各国政府都在做出忽视基础设施建设现实的政治承诺。“他们没有为自己的社区进行治理，而是选择了人工智能的叙述。”一位为多个人工智能增长区项目提供咨询服务的英国工程咨询公司分析师表示：“对于这些具有全国意义的项目，似乎没有进行适当的审查，无论是公开的还是其他方面的。许多方案背后的数据和设计往好了说是指示性的，往坏了说就是完全是废话。”从“无计划”到英国最大的陆上风电场，拉纳克郡综合体的开发商DataVita表示，将为艾尔德里的风电场提供超过1GW的可再生能源，其中包括400MW的太阳能和800MW的风能。这是英国最大的陆上风电场Whitelee生产的风能的一倍半以上。占地相当于布里斯托尔一半的面积，大约可以满足 80 万户苏格兰家庭的供电需求。DataVita 在其网站上暗示，所有能源都已经就位，并描述了与其数据中心“直接连接”的“能源园区”，提供相当于小型核反应堆的电力。但没有证据表明 DataVita 目前拥有 1GW 的专线可再生能源为其数据中心供电，也没有证据表明它会在不久的将来拥有这一电力。目前，该公司拥有两个规模小得多的运营数据中心，一个位于格拉斯哥，一个位于拉纳克郡数据中心枢纽所在地，DataVita 没有回应有关其当前是否安装可再生能源的询问。该网站的能源计划包括 400MW 太阳能和 800MW 风能。照片：Murdo MacLeod/The Guardian 也几乎没有证据表明 DataVita 会拥有这一项目。由于没有必要的土地，《卫报》根据爱丁堡慈善机构保护苏格兰乡村行动 (APRS) 的数据进行了分析，并经过一位从事数据中心工作的能源顾问的审查，表明 DataVita 的可再生能源计划将需要 40 至 100 平方公里的土地。根据《卫报》审查的 APRS 网站上的分析，其目前提交的规划申请覆盖拉纳克郡约 2 平方公里。 DataVita 声称拥有“超过 1,000 英亩”的可再生能源，即 4 平方公里。审查这些数据的能源顾问表示：“在四年内从‘无公共设施’到‘全国最大的运营陆上风电场’是相当雄心勃勃的。”鉴于 DataVita 没有此类开发计划，他的结论是 2030 年的最后期限不太可能实现。 DataVita 的母公司 HfD 集团最近公布了在拉纳克郡建设“能源公园”的计划。这些计划似乎尚未进入规划流程，但设想“多达 19 个”风力涡轮机

articles.title: ITV sells media and entertainment arm to Sky for £1.6bn
articles.summary: The two broadcasters say the deal will create a strong rival to global streaming giants.
articles.titleZh: ITV 以 16 亿英镑将媒体和娱乐部门出售给 Sky
articles.summaryZh: 两家广播公司表示，这笔交易将为全球流媒体巨头带来强大的竞争对手。

contentParagraphs.en: ITV is selling its media and entertainment divisions to Sky in a £1.6bn deal that the companies say will create a strong rival to global streaming giants.The sale includes ITV's broadcast channels and its ITVX streaming service, with Sky's chief executive Dana Strong calling it "a defining moment for British media".It is one of the biggest takeovers in British media history, with American-owned Comcast - which owns Sky - having started takeover talks in November last year.ITV's studio arm, which makes popular programmes such as Love Island and I'm a Celebrity... Get Me Out of Here, is not included in the deal.The deal means Sky will get access to millions more viewers, and as well as scale and prominence on a free-to-air platform.Viewers are unlikely to notice any difference initially. The takeover will not lead to ITV's shows being moved behind a paywall for now as ITV is required by law to provide a free-to-air service until at least 2034 under its public service broadcasting licence.In a statement, Sky Group said there would be no immediate change to popular shows."The UK media market is undergoing a profound and rapid transformation, and as competition for audiences intensifies, scale matters more than ever in order to compete with global streaming giants and YouTube in the UK," Sky said ."Viewers will continue to enjoy the shows they know and love, such as Coronation Street, Emmerdale, Love Island, I'm a Celebrity... Get Me Out of Here!, This Morning, Loose Women, Lorraine and News at Ten – alongside major live sporting events."Former ITV chairman Sir Peter Bazalgette, who owns shares in ITV, said the deal was "essential" for the survival of the broadcasters."If we don't see consolidation between domestic broadcasters, we won't have any in 20 years time and it's the same for all the European countries because of the competition from the streamers," he told the BBC's Today programme."It's a good deal for viewers because it sustains, will sustain, ITV's investment – as its obligations are as a public service broadcaster – in international news, national news, regional news and all the programmes that its viewers love", he said.Sir Peter added that consolidating channels in the UK would put them in a better position to compete against global players, as the sheer size of the US media market gave its firms huge financial firepower.The takeover is still subject to approval from regulators, but when it is completed ITV Studios will become a standalone business.Under the terms of the deal, ITV will receive £1.2bn in cash and Sky's Love Productions business, whose shows include Great British Bake Off, which is valued at £200m. ITV will also get a further £200m in 2028 if it meets advertising revenue targets.Sky said it had also agreed to spend £2.1bn on content from ITV Studios over a five-year period.Susannah Streeter, chief investment strategist at Wealth Club, said the deal would be a "significant step in the reshaping of Europe's media landscape"."Traditional broadcasters are having to change tactics fast in the battle for audiences whose attention is increasingly fragmented across streaming platforms, social media and online video, making advertising revenues harder to sustain," she said.
contentParagraphs.zh: ITV 将以 16 亿英镑的价格将其媒体和娱乐部门出售给 Sky，两家公司表示，这将成为全球流媒体巨头的强大竞争对手。此次出售包括 ITV 的广播频道及其 ITVX 流媒体服务，Sky 首席执行官达纳·斯特朗 (Dana Strong) 称其为“英国媒体的决定性时刻”。这是英国媒体历史上最大的收购之一，美国拥有的康卡斯特（Sky 的所有者）已于去年 11 月开始收购谈判。诸如《爱情岛》和《我是名人...让我离开这里》等热门节目不包含在交易中。这笔交易意味着天空电视台将获得数以百万计的更多观众，并在免费平台上扩大规模和知名度。观众最初不太可能注意到任何差异。此次收购不会导致 ITV 的节目暂时转入付费专区，因为法律要求 ITV 根据其公共服务广播许可证至少在 2034 年之前提供免费服务。 Sky Group 在一份声明中表示，热门节目不会立即发生变化。“英国媒体市场正在经历深刻而快速的转型，随着观众竞争的加剧，为了与全球流媒体巨头和英国 YouTube 竞争，规模比以往任何时候都更加重要。” ” 说。“观众将继续欣赏他们熟悉和喜爱的节目，例如《加冕街》、《爱默代尔》、《爱情岛》、《我是名人...让我离开这里！》、《今晨》、《松散的女人》、《洛林》和《十点新闻》——以及主要的现场体育赛事。”他告诉 BBC 的《今日》节目，“这对观众来说是一笔好交易，因为它维持、将会维持 ITV 在国际新闻、国家新闻、地区新闻和所有观众喜爱的节目方面的投资，因为它作为公共服务广播公司的义务。”由于美国媒体市场的庞大规模为其公司提供了巨大的财务实力，因此他们在与全球竞争对手的竞争中处于更有利的地位。此次收购仍需获得监管机构的批准，但完成后，ITV Studios 将成为一家独立的企业。根据交易条款，ITV 将获得 12 亿英镑现金和 Sky 的 Love Productions 业务，该业务的节目包括价值 2 亿英镑的 Great Britain Bake Off。如果ITV达到广告收入目标，它还将在2028年获得另外2亿英镑。Sky表示，它还同意在五年内斥资21亿英镑购买ITV Studios的内容。Wealth Club首席投资策略师Susannah Streeter表示，这笔交易将是“重塑欧洲媒体版图的重要一步”。“传统广播公司在争夺观众注意力的斗争中必须迅速改变策略流媒体平台、社交媒体和在线视频日益分散，使得广告收入更难以维持，”她说。

articles.title: A rare flower is in a superbloom in the Rocky Mountains
articles.summary: Green gentian wildflowers are in a major superbloom in the alpine meadows of Colorado. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to David Inouye, a researcher who has been studying these magnificent flowers for decades.
articles.titleZh: 落基山脉中一朵罕见的花朵盛开
articles.summaryZh: 科罗拉多州高山草甸上的绿色龙胆野花正盛开。 NPR 的阿伊莎·拉斯科 (Ayesha Rascoe) 采访了几十年来一直在研究这些美丽花朵的研究员大卫·井上 (David Inouye)。

contentParagraphs.en: A rare flower is in a superbloom in the Rocky Mountains
contentParagraphs.zh: 落基山脉的超级花朵中绽放着一朵罕见的花朵

contentParagraphs.en: Green gentian wildflowers are in a major superbloom in the alpine meadows of Colorado. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to David Inouye, a researcher who has been studying these magnificent flowers for decades.
contentParagraphs.zh: 绿色龙胆野花在科罗拉多州的高山草甸中盛开。NPR的Ayesha Rascoe采访了几十年来一直在研究这些壮丽花卉的研究员David Inouye。

contentParagraphs.zh: 落基山脉的超级花朵中绽放着一朵罕见的花朵

contentParagraphs.en: <iframe src="https://www.npr.org/player/embed/nx-s1-5877757/nx-s1-9836737" width="100%" height="290" frameborder="0" scrolling="no" title="NPR embedded audio player">

contentParagraphs.en: Green gentian wildflowers are in a major superbloom in the alpine meadows of Colorado. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to David Inouye, a researcher who has been studying these magnificent flowers for decades.
contentParagraphs.zh: 绿色龙胆野花在科罗拉多州的高山草甸中盛开。NPR的Ayesha Rascoe采访了几十年来一直在研究这些壮丽花卉的研究员David Inouye。

contentParagraphs.en: AYESHA RASCOE, HOST: This summer, Mother Nature has decided to put on a rare show in the Rocky Mountains. A very special wildflower, the green gentians, are in a major super bloom in the alpine meadows of Colorado. But these blooms are hard-won. The gentians grow for decades and bloom briefly. Then they die, never to bloom again. David Inouye is a researcher at the Rocky Mountain Biological Laboratory. He's been studying green gentians since the '70s, and he joins us now. Welcome, David.DAVID INOUYE: Thank you. I'm happy to be here.RASCOE: So take me back to 1973. There was a green gentian bloom like this one that caused you to start studying these flowers. What did you see?INOUYE: Well, some previous work that had been done here on these gentians, they found lots and lots of plants in one year, and then they came back to study them the next year, and there were almost no flowering plants. And Chip Taylor from the University of Kansas and I decided that we would try and discover what it was that created this kind of off-year and then on-year flowering pattern. And we started tagging several hundred plants in 1973 that we could come back and find again. And I've kept that study going now since 1973. So some of the plants that we're following, we've been looking at for over 50 years, and some of those plants have still not yet flowered up at the...RASCOE: Oh, wow.INOUYE: ...Higher altitudes.RASCOE: Wow. They still haven't flowered over 50 years?INOUYE: That's right. And in, see, 1982, I planted some seeds, and the first one to flower was 20 years later in 2003. And then after 40 years, I think all of them have finally flowered and died.RASCOE: Why do they take so long to bloom? Like, what are they doing all that time?INOUYE: They're stirring up energy in a root underground. And then in the last year of their life, they use all that energy to put this big flower stalk up. On average, the plants are, oh, about 5 feet tall, although I have seen them as tall as 9 feet tall. If you think about it, a plant that only has one chance in its life to flower and it wants to attract pollinators, wants to make itself conspicuous. And so one way it does that is to make sure that it sticks up higher than the surrounding vegetation.RASCOE: Since they take so long to grow, how do you know when they're going to bloom?INOUYE: Well, it turns out that these plants do something called preformation. They actually start making their leaves four years before those leaves appear above ground, and that means that they're also preforming their flower stalks. So I think what's happening is if we have an unusually wet summer, that triggers some of these plants to stop making leaves underground and start making the beginning of a flower stalk. Last summer was quite a dry summer. I know that four years from last summer is not going to be a good flowering year. That also means that if a climate is changing and becoming drier, these flowering events are going to be happening less frequently. It's been seven years since the last one, and those intervals between these big super blooms could start becoming longer and longer as the climate dries out.RASCOE: That's David Inouye, a researcher at the Rocky Mountain Biological Laboratory. Thank you so much.INOUYE: You're quite welcome.(SOUNDBITE OF THE HIP ABDUCTION'S "ALPINE FLOWERS")

articles.title: Huge crowd joins funeral procession for Iran’s Supreme Leader Khamenei
articles.summary: Enormous crowds gather in Tehran for third day to mourn amid calls for revenge against the US and Israel.
articles.titleZh: 大批民众参加伊朗最高领袖哈梅内伊的送葬队伍
articles.summaryZh: 大量人群连续第三天聚集在德黑兰哀悼，呼吁对美国和以色列进行报复。

contentParagraphs.en: Enormous crowds gather in Tehran for third day to mourn as authorities and Khamenei’s supporters call for revenge against the US and Israel.The funeral procession for Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei is under way in Tehran as authorities have hailed the government’s resilience during the United States-Israel war on Iran and promised vengeance for his assassination.After lying in state for two days at Tehran’s Grand Mosalla religious complex, Khamenei’s body began a 12-hour journey on Monday through the capital, accompanied by large crowds of mourners.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3What is the religious and political messaging behind Khamenei’s funeral?list 2 of 3Photos: Millions mourn Iran’s Ali Khamenei amid historic funeral processionlist 3 of 3Iran promotes message of continuity and revenge at Khamenei commemorationend of listKhamenei, 86, was killed in an air strike on February 28, the first day of the war, after ruling Iran for nearly 37 years.Mourners gathered in Imam Hussein Square in eastern Tehran and hanged an effigy of US President Donald Trump, according to state media.Others carried placards with images of US Vice President JD Vance, US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and the words: “There will be blood.”Thousands filled the Grand Mosalla on Sunday to pay their respects to Khamenei. The other remains on display were Khamenei’s daughter, son-in-law, daughter-in-law and 14-month-old granddaughter who were all killed alongside him.As well as laying Khamenei to rest, Iran’s authorities are using the weeklong funeral ceremonies to promote their resilience during a ceasefire with the US.Iran’s parliament speaker and chief negotiator with the US, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, hailed on social media the way the “proud and invincible nation of Islamic Iran unanimously” paid tribute to its “martyr”.Monday’s procession will be followed by similar events in the clerical hub of Qom on Tuesday and in Iraq’s holy cities of Najaf and Karbala on Wednesday, culminating in Khamenei’s burial in his hometown of Mashhad in northeastern Iran on Thursday.Three of Khamenei’s sons made a rare public appearance at the funeral on Sunday, further highlighting the absence of Mojtaba Khamenei, who was named supreme leader shortly after his father’s killing but has yet to appear in public.Officials have said he was wounded in the air strikes that killed his father, but the severity of his injuries remains unclear.While Iranian authorities have been eager to present a united front, none of President Masoud Pezeshkian’s surviving predecessors, who had tense relations with Khamenei, have so far been seen at the ceremonies.The US-Israel war on Iran has been halted during the ceasefire with the US. Both Washington and Tehran have warned they are ready to resume military action, and vengeance has been a major theme at the weeklong ceremonies for Ali Khamenei.Iran’s army chief, Major General Amir Hatami, pledged to never abandon a quest for justice.“Those who committed this crime must know that the nation of Iran and all of us will never cease in our pursuit of and demand for justice,” Hatami said, according to Iran’s state-run Press TV.“The killers [of Khamenei] must face punishment,” a 38-year-old man who gave his surname as Miremadi told the AFP news agency at the prayers on Sunday.Khamenei long pursued a course of confrontation with the West. Tehran has for years provided support to anti-US and anti-Israel armed groups operating around the Middle East, including the Palestinian group Hamas and Lebanon’s Hezbollah, which both sent delegations to the ceremonies.
contentParagraphs.zh: 大量人群连续第三天聚集在德黑兰哀悼，当局和哈梅内伊的支持者呼吁对美国和以色列进行报复。伊朗最高领袖阿里·哈梅内伊的葬礼队伍正在德黑兰进行，当局赞扬了政府在美以对伊朗战争期间的复原力，并承诺对哈梅内伊被暗杀进行复仇。在德黑兰大摩萨拉宗教建筑群安放了两天后，哈梅内伊的遗体开始了葬礼。周一，在大批哀悼者的陪伴下，经过首都 12 小时的旅程。推荐故事 列表 3 项列表 1（共 3） 哈梅内伊葬礼背后的宗教和政治信息是什么？列表 2（共 3）照片：数百万人在历史性的葬礼游行中哀悼伊朗阿里·哈梅内伊列表 3（共 3）伊朗在哈梅内伊纪念活动中宣扬延续性和复仇的信息列表结束86 岁的哈梅内伊在空中被杀2 月 28 日，即战争的第一天，统治伊朗近 37 年之后，发生了袭击。据官方媒体报道，哀悼者聚集在德黑兰东部的伊玛目侯赛因广场，悬挂了美国总统唐纳德·特朗普的肖像。其他人举着标语牌，上面有美国副总统万斯、美国国防部长皮特·赫格斯和以色列总理本杰明·内塔尼亚胡的照片，上面写着：“将会有血。”数千人挤满了大摩萨拉教堂周日向哈梅内伊致敬。展出的其他遗骸包括哈梅内伊的女儿、女婿、儿媳和 14 个月大的孙女，她们都与哈梅内伊一起被杀。除了安息哈梅内伊外，伊朗当局还利用为期一周的葬礼来增强其在与美国停火期间的韧性。伊朗议会议长兼与美国的首席谈判代表穆罕默德·巴格尔·加利巴夫在社交媒体上称赞伊朗“自豪的”方式。和不可战胜的伊斯兰国家伊朗一致”向其“烈士”致敬。周一的游行之后，周二在库姆的文职中心以及周三在伊拉克圣城纳杰夫和卡尔巴拉举行类似的活动，最终哈梅内伊于周四在伊朗东北部的家乡马什哈德下葬。哈梅内伊的三个儿子在周日的葬礼上罕见地公开露面，进一步凸显了哈梅内伊的缺席。莫吉塔巴·哈梅内伊在其父亲被杀后不久被任命为最高领袖，但尚未公开露面。官员称，他在导致其父亲死亡的空袭中受伤，但伤势严重程度仍不清楚。虽然伊朗当局一直渴望建立统一战线，但与哈梅内伊关系紧张的总统马苏德·佩泽什基安的幸存前任迄今尚未出现在仪式上。在与美国停火期间，有关伊朗的行动已停止。华盛顿和德黑兰都警告说，他们已准备好恢复军事行动，而复仇一直是阿里·哈梅内伊为期一周的仪式的一个主题。伊朗陆军参谋长阿米尔·哈塔米少将承诺永远不会放弃对正义的追求。据伊朗国营新闻电视台报道，哈塔米说：“犯下这一罪行的人必须知道，伊朗国家和我们所有人将永远不会停止对正义的追求和要求。”一位姓米雷马迪的 38 岁男子在周日的祈祷中告诉法新社：“哈梅内伊]必须受到惩罚。”哈梅内伊长期以来一直奉行与西方对抗的方针。德黑兰多年来一直为在中东地区活动的反美和反以色列武装组织提供支持，其中包括巴勒斯坦组织哈马斯和黎巴嫩真主党，它们都派代表团参加了这些仪式。

articles.title: China wants to solve the hardest problem in robotics – making hands
articles.summary:
Race to develop ‘embodied AI’ focuses on creating dextrous hands to transform humanoid robots from gimmicks into useful products
Human hands – nimble, nerve-filled appendages that are the most flexible part of the human skeleton – are exceptionally complex. Many tasks that most p
articles.titleZh: 中国希望解决机器人技术中最难的问题——制造双手
articles.summaryZh:
开发“嵌入式人工智能”的竞赛重点是创造灵巧的双手，将人形机器人从噱头转变为有用的产品
人手是灵活、充满神经的附肢，是人体骨骼中最灵活的部分，而且异常复杂。大多数任务

contentParagraphs.en: Human hands – nimble, nerve-filled appendages that are the most flexible part of the human skeleton – are exceptionally complex. Many tasks that most people can do largely without thinking, from tying a pair of shoelaces to buttoning up a shirt, in fact require a complex set of neurological instructions and precise choreography. In thousands of years of human history, no machine has been able to truly replicate human’s greatest tool.But now, as artificial intelligence (AI) races forwards, some companies think they are close to surpassing this final but most difficult hurdle in robotics. Most of them are in China.A new suite of Chinese start-ups are leveraging China’s advantages in manufacturing and enthusiasm for what the government calls “embodied AI” to build the fully dextrous robotic hands that are needed to transform humanoid robots from dancing gimmicks into useful products.Ever since Unitree’s troupe of dancing humanoids tottered on to the stage at 2025’s Spring Gala, the annual variety show televised at Lunar New Year, China has been going gaga for robots.Technologists and policymakers see robotics as the key to unlocking China’s future economic potential as it grapples with an ageing and shrinking workforce. Marketing materials for robotic companies show their products performing all kinds of tasks that humans will supposedly soon be free of: folding laundry, cooking, cutting hair.A humanoid robot wearing a traditional mamianqun skirt strikes a Chinese drum at the Linkerbot office in May in Beijing. Photograph: Emmanuel Wong/The GuardianBeijing has repeatedly emphasised the importance of “embodied AI” in China’s development plans. In May, the Chinese Communist party’s theoretical journal, Qiushi, published a report that said “embodied-intelligence robots” were among the sectors “opening up new trillion-yuan markets”.But although China is racing ahead in the deployment of automatons – more than half of the factory robots that are installed each year are in China – the use cases for humanoids remain minimal. “True multipurpose humanoids are far off yet,” concluded the International Federation of Robotics in a report published last September.That is because many of the tasks that would make humanoids useful in daily settings require human-like hands. And making them is extremely difficult. Last year, Elon Musk, whose company Tesla makes the Optimus humanoid, said hands represented the “majority of the engineering difficulty of the entire robot”.‘100 times more difficult’In an office brimming with writhing, floating robotic hands of various weights and sizes, the founder of LinkerBot, one of China’s leading dextrous hands companies, explains the challenge.Making a robotic hand is “one hundred times more difficult” than making a humanoid, Zhou Yong says. “Its dexterity is 10 times that of other body parts. But its volume is only one tenth of other body parts”.Alex Zhou, founder of tech startup Linkerbot, poses for a photo alongside a humanoid robot and a robotic dextrous hand at the company’s office in May in Beijing. Photograph: Emmanuel Wong/The GuardianLike many Chinese entrepreneurs, Zhou is inspired by the American greats. Having graduated from Huazhong University of Science and Technology, one of China’s top schools, Zhou was interested in designing apps as well as robotics. But he listened to the quote from Steve Jobs about the importance of focus (“Innovation is saying no to 1,000 things”) and decided to focus just on hands, launching LinkerBot in 2023. The company now makes about 5,000 hands a month and has plans to double that figure as it chases a valuation of $6bn. “Human hands are the most important ability of human beings,” Zhou says. “If we focus on this one point, it is easier to realise many human skills.”Among Zhou’s ambitions are to make mass-market prosthetic hands for amputees at a fraction of the current price, which can be tens of thousands of dollars. Zhou believes his company will be able to bring the price down to just $1,000 per hand.Making robotic hands requires solving hardware and software problems.A technician calibrates a robotic dextrous hand mounted on a mechanical arm at Linkerbot. Photograph: Emmanuel Wong/The GuardianThanks to a cheap, sophisticated and nimble manufacturing supply chain, Chinese companies are racing ahead on the hardware side. The rise of China’s electric vehicle industry has produced many companies that are capable of producing the components needs for robots at scale, from lithium-ion batteries to miniaturised motors.Pan Yunzhe, the founder of Wuji Technology, a Shenzhen-based robotic hands company, says the ease of sourcing components in China is the reason he founded his company there rather than in the US, where he graduated in 2018.Humanoid robots playing music“It was really impossible to do hardware in the United States because the supply chain problem is just so constraining,” he says. When he tried to start a company in the US, he needed to ask his father to post parts to him. So he decided to return to China to start his company there instead.Teaching hands to moveZhou and Pan are two of the thousands of entrepreneurs betting on China’s robotics hype. China has now registered more than 1m robotic companies, with registrations in 2025 up 40% on the previous year. The companies focused only on hands represent a fraction of that market, but it is growing fast. Last year the dextrous hand industry in China surpassed 50bn yuan ($7.4bn), according to Chinese media, up from 13bn yuan in 2024.Pan says he decided to focus on hands because “the problem of manipulation is much more important than the problem of locomotion”. Humanoids can move through space, but until they can manipulate tools, they are all but useless.The more challenging problem is software – how to teach the hands how to do things.“The challenge of making these hands is getting solved now,” says Nathan Lepora, a professor of robotics and AI at the University of
contentParagraphs.zh: 人手是灵活、充满神经的附肢，是人体骨骼中最灵活的部分，而且异常复杂。大多数人无需思考就能完成的许多任务，从系鞋带到扣衬衫扣子，实际上都需要一套复杂的神经指令和精确的编排。在人类几千年的历史中，没有任何机器能够真正复制人类最伟大的工具。但现在，随着人工智能（AI）的快速发展，一些公司认为他们已经接近跨越机器人技术中最后但最困难的障碍。其中大多数都在中国。一批新的中国初创企业正在利用中国的制造业优势和政府对“嵌入式人工智能”的热情，打造完全灵巧的机械手，将人形机器人从跳舞的噱头转变为有用的产品。自从 Unitree 的人形舞蹈团在 2025 年农历新年电视晚会这一年度综艺节目上登上舞台以来，中国一直在技术专家和政策制定者将机器人视为释放中国未来经济潜力的关键，因为中国正在应对劳动力老龄化和萎缩的问题。机器人公司的营销材料显示，他们的产品可以执行人类很快就能摆脱的各种任务：叠衣服、做饭、理发。五月份，一个穿着传统马面裙的人形机器人在北京 Linkerbot 办公室敲击中国鼓。照片：Emmanuel Wong/卫报北京多次强调“嵌入式人工智能”在中国发展计划中的重要性。今年5月，中国共产党的理论刊物《求是》发表了一篇报告，称“具身智能机器人”是“开辟万亿级新市场”的行业之一。不过，尽管中国在机器人的部署方面遥遥领先——每年安装的工厂机器人有一半以上在中国——但类人机器人的使用案例仍然很少。国际机器人联合会在去年 9 月发布的一份报告中总结道：“真正的多用途类人机器人还很遥远。”这是因为使类人机器人在日常环境中发挥作用的许多任务都需要类人的双手。制作它们极其困难。去年，特斯拉公司生产擎天柱人形机器人的埃隆·马斯克表示，手代表了“整个机器人工程难度的大部分”。“难度是其一百倍”。中国领先的灵巧手公司之一 LinkerBot 的创始人在办公室里摆满了不同重量和大小的扭动、漂浮的机器人手，他解释了这一挑战。周勇说，制造机械手比制造人形机器人“困难一百倍”。 “它的灵巧度是身体其他部位的 10 倍，但体积却只有身体其他部位的十分之一。”科技初创公司 Linkerbot 的创始人 Alex Zhou 5 月在北京公司办公室与人形机器人和机器人灵巧手合影。摄影：Emmanuel Wong/卫报 像许多中国企业家一样，周也受到美国伟人的启发。周毕业于中国顶尖学府之一的华中科技大学，对设计应用程序和机器人技术很感兴趣。但他听了史蒂夫·乔布斯关于专注重要性的名言（“创新就是对 1,000 件事说不”），并决定只专注于手，并于 2023 年推出了 LinkerBot。该公司现在每月生产约 5,000 手，并计划在追求 60 亿美元估值时将这一数字翻倍。 “人的手是人类最重要的能力，”周说。 “如果我们专注于这一点，就更容易实现许多人类技能。” 周的目标之一是以当前价格的一小部分（可能是数万美元）为截肢者制造大众市场的假肢。周相信他的公司将能够将价格降至每只手 1,000 美元。制造机械手需要解决硬件和软件问题。技术人员正在校准 Linkerbot 机械臂上安装的机械手。摄影：Emmanuel Wong/卫报 得益于廉价、先进且灵活的制造供应链，中国企业在硬件方面遥遥领先。中国电动汽车行业的崛起催生了许多有能力大规模生产机器人所需零部件的公司，从锂离子电池到微型电机。深圳机械手公司无极科技的创始人潘云哲表示，在中国采购零部件很方便，这是他在中国而不是在美国创办公司的原因，他于2018年毕业于美国。限制，”他说。当他试图在美国创办一家公司时，他需要让父亲寄零件给他。于是，他决定返回中国，在那里创办自己的公司。 手把手教感动 周和潘是数千名押注中国机器人炒作的企业家中的两人。目前，中国注册机器人企业已超过100万家，2025年注册数量较上年增长40%。只专注于手的公司只占该市场的一小部分，但它正在快速增长。据中国媒体报道，去年中国灵巧手行业的产值超过了 500 亿元人民币（合 74 亿美元），而 2024 年这一数字为 130 亿元人民币。潘说，他决定把重点放在手上，因为“操纵问题比运动问题重要得多”。类人机器人可以在太空中移动，但在它们能够操纵工具之前，它们几乎毫无用处。更具挑战性的问题是软件——如何教双手如何做事。“制造这些手的挑战现在已经得到解决，”美国大学机器人学和人工智能教授内森·莱波拉 (Nathan Lepora) 说。

articles.title: Backlash after China bubble tea firm ordered to pay Louis Vuitton $1.5m
articles.summary: A court in China ruled that Molly Tea had infringed on the luxury brand's four-petal flower design.
articles.titleZh: 中国珍珠奶茶公司被勒令向路易威登支付 150 万美元后遭到强烈反对
articles.summaryZh: 中国一家法院裁定莫莉茶侵犯了该奢侈品牌的四瓣花设计。

contentParagraphs.en: Popular Chinese tea chain Molly Tea has been ordered to pay 10.3m yuan (£1.1m; $1.5m) in damages after a court ruled that its logo infringed a Louis Vuitton trademark, fuelling an online debate over intellectual property protection.Chinese media reported last week that a court in the eastern Jiangsu province ruled that the Shenzhen-based tea company had copied Louis Vuitton's iconic four-petal flower design.The decision has divided the public online in China, with a hashtag linked to the case drawing more than 400 million views and tens of thousands of comments.The BBC has contacted Molly Tea and Louis Vuitton for comment.On Thursday, a court in Suzhou, just east of Shanghai, ordered Molly Tea to stop using of the logo, issue a public apology and to pay damages to Louis Vuitton, according to Chinese state media China Daily.The outlet also said that Molly Tea and its affiliated firms had applied for multiple trademarks that were rejected by the China National Intellectual Property Administration.Only the trademark containing the Chinese characters for "Molly Tea" was successfully registered, China Daily reported.Many Chinese social media users have defended the design of Molly Tea's logo, noting how many designs used in Western luxury brands have been inspired by Chinese artefacts.One commenter on the Weibo platform wrote in Mandarin that he will "drink a cup of Molly Tea daily" to show his support for the company."Give me a break. They're just taking advantage of the fact that our ancestors didn't file for patents," the commenter wrote.One user on RedNote, another Chinese social media platform, said: "Such basic geometric shapes have been used everywhere throughout history, not just China."But some online supported the court's verdict.A Weibo user said that those who back Molly Tea's design should "study law first", arguing that there is no dispute as Louis Vuitton had already registered the logo.Another said Louis Vuitton is justified in defending its intellectual property and that other brands do not have the right to imitate it, regardless of their industry.
contentParagraphs.zh: 法院裁定其标志侵犯路易威登商标后，中国受欢迎的茶叶连锁店茉莉茶被责令支付 1030 万元人民币（110 万英镑；150 万美元）的赔偿金，引发了网上有关知识产权保护的争论。中国媒体上周报道称，江苏省东部的一家法院裁定这家总部位于深圳的茶叶公司抄袭了路易威登标志性的四瓣花设计。这一裁决在中国网上引起了公众的分歧，有一个主题标签与该案吸引了超过 4 亿次观看和数万条评论。英国广播公司 (BBC) 已联系 Molly Tea 和路易威登发表评论。据中国官方媒体《中国日报》报道，周四，上海以东的苏州一家法院下令 Molly Tea 停止使用该标志，公开道歉并向路易威登支付赔偿金。该媒体还称，Molly Tea 及其附属公司已申请多个商标，但均被中国国家知识产权局驳回。据《中国日报》报道，包含“莫莉茶”汉字的商标已成功注册。许多中国社交媒体用户为莫莉茶的标志设计进行了辩护，指出许多西方奢侈品牌的设计灵感都来自中国文物。微博平台上的一位评论者用普通话写道，他将“每天喝一杯莫莉茶”以表达对公司的支持。“让我休息一下。他们只是在利用我们的祖先没有申请商标的事实。”另一家中国社交媒体平台红笔记上的一位用户表示：“这种基本的几何形状在历史上到处都在使用，不仅仅是中国。”但网上也有一些人支持法院的判决。一位微博用户表示，那些支持莫莉茶设计的人应该“先学法律”，认为路易威登已经注册了该标志，因此没有争议。另一位用户则表示，路易威登捍卫其知识产权是正当的，其他品牌没有模仿的权利，无论其行业如何。

contentParagraphs.en: If you miss your flight due to EES queues, airlines are under no obligation to put you on another flight without an extra charge.Travel insurance is unlikely to cover the cost of new flights, hotels or any other losses incurred as a result of delays caused by EES queues, the Association of British Insurers says.Anne Robinson, from Dunbarton, and her 13 year-old-son Jack missed their flight home from Rome in June when they got stuck in a 90-minute queue. They had to pay £250 for a replacement flight two days later, which they could not claim back.No travel insurance policies explicitly cover EES delays, because it is an expected procedure or routine process, explains Anna-Marie Duthie, travel insurance expert at analysts Defaqto."In practice it is your responsibility to allow additional time for such long queues or delays," she says.There is a slight chance of a claim under missed departure, or delay or abandonment, cover. However, immigration or border delays are often not listed covered reasons, she says.Top tip: Read your paperwork or speak to your insurer before you travel.
contentParagraphs.zh: 如果您因 EES 排队而错过航班，航空公司没有义务为您安排另一趟航班，且不收取额外费用。英国保险公司协会表示，旅行保险不太可能承保新航班、酒店费用或因 EES 排队延误而造成的任何其他损失。来自邓巴顿的安妮·罗宾逊 (Anne Robinson) 和她 13 岁的儿子杰克 6 月份因排队 90 分钟而错过了从罗马回家的航班。他们必须为两天后的替代航班支付 250 英镑，但无法索赔。没有任何旅行保险政策明确涵盖 EES 延误，因为这是预期的程序或例行程序，分析公司 Defaqto 的旅行保险专家 Anna-Marie Duthie 解释道。“实际上，您有责任为如此长的排队或延误留出额外的时间，”她说。根据错过出发、延误或遗弃保险提出索赔的可能性很小。然而，她说，移民或边境延误通常不会列出承保原因。重要提示：在旅行前阅读您的文件或与您的保险公司联系。

articles.title: Impeachment trial of Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte begins
articles.summary: Senate trial outcome could permanently bar Duterte from office, shaking her frontrunner status for the 2028 election.
articles.titleZh: 菲律宾副总统萨拉·杜特尔特弹劾审判开始
articles.summaryZh: 参议院的审判结果可能会永久禁止杜特尔特任职，从而动摇她在 2028 年选举中的领先地位。

contentParagraphs.en: Senate trial outcome could permanently bar Duterte from office, shaking her frontrunner status for the 2028 election.The impeachment trial of Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte has begun in Manila, in a highly charged case that could determine her political future.Thousands of police were deployed around the Senate in the capital on Monday as protesters calling for Duterte’s conviction began gathering outside. The vice president’s office said she would not appear in person.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3Philippine Congress impeaches VP Sara Duterte for second timelist 2 of 3Philippine lawmakers impeach Vice President Sara Dutertelist 3 of 3Philippines launches VP Duterte impeachment trial amid political divisionend of listThe House of Representatives impeached the daughter of former President Rodrigo Duterte on allegations of corruption, bribery and an assassination plot against one-time ally President Ferdinand Marcos. Duterte denies wrongdoing.“We, her lawyers, are here … to prove the allegations against her have no basis,” Michael Poa of Duterte’s defence team told reporters.In a statement, Duterte said the decision to “appear through counsel rather than testify personally does not diminish accountability or imply a lack of transparency”.Second impeachment complaintThe outcome of the trial, which could last several months, may determine whether Duterte will be barred from running for president in the 2028 election.Only a guilty verdict by two-thirds of the bitterly divided 24-seat Senate can strip her of the vice presidency and permanently bar her from elected office.Duterte remains a presidential frontrunner for 2028, with a late May survey suggesting 51 percent of respondents planned to vote for her.Reporting from Manila, Al Jazeera’s Barnaby Lo said less than two years into the Marcos administration, the House of Representatives – then led by a cousin of the president – began investigating the vice president for alleged corruption.“The probe snowballed into the first impeachment complaint … which was voided by the Supreme Court,” he said. “But here we are now, with Duterte facing a second impeachment complaint.”Duterte and Marcos are heirs to two of the country’s most powerful political dynasties.In 2022, they won an election on a joint ticket, but their alliance has since collapsed in an increasingly bitter feud that deepened after ⁠Rodrigo Duterte’s arrest and transfer to International Criminal Court (ICC) custody last year on murder charges linked to his so-called “war on drugs”.The bitter fight has spilled into the Senate, raising questions about how the trial will unfold.In a dramatic turn on Monday, Duterte’s ally Senator Rodante Marcoleta was arrested on a plunder charge shortly before the start of the trial, throwing support for the vice president in the Senate into doubt.In May, just as the Senate was preparing to receive the impeachment complaint from the House of ⁠Representatives, Duterte ally Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa turned up in the chamber after being absent from public view ⁠since November and cast the decisive vote to install Alan Peter Cayetano as Senate president.Dela Rosa then holed up in the Senate before slipping away early on May 14, hours after chaos and gunfire erupted in the parliament building. His whereabouts are unknown. Cayetano was the running mate of Duterte’s father in the 2016 election.Dela Rosa, who also faces ICC charges, was national police chief during much of the drug crackdown, in which thousands of suspects were killed. He and Rodrigo Duterte have denied wrongdoing.About a month later, senators aligned with a rival bloc secured enough support to elect Senator Sherwin Gatchalian as Senate president.
contentParagraphs.zh: 参议院审判结果可能会永久禁止杜特尔特上任，从而动摇她在 2028 年大选中的领先地位。菲律宾副总统萨拉·杜特尔特的弹劾审判已在马尼拉开始，这是一个高度紧张的案件，可能决定她的政治未来。周一，数千名警察部署在首都参议院周围，要求定罪杜特尔特的抗议者开始在外面聚集。副总统办公室表示她不会亲自出庭。 推荐故事 3 项清单 清单 1（共 3 项）菲律宾国会第二次弹劾副总统萨拉·杜特尔特（清单 3 名议员中的 2 名）菲律宾议员弹劾副总统萨拉·杜特尔特（清单 3 项）菲律宾在政治分歧中启动副总统杜特尔特弹劾审判清单结束众议院弹劾前总统罗德里戈·杜特尔特的女儿，指控其腐败、贿赂和针对曾经的盟友费迪南德·马科斯总统的暗杀阴谋。杜特尔特否认有不当行为。杜特尔特辩护团队的迈克尔·波阿 (Michael Poa) 告诉记者：“我们，她的律师，在这里……证明对她的指控没有根据。”杜特尔特在一份声明中表示，“通过律师出庭而不是亲自作证，并不会减少责任，也不会意味着缺乏透明度。”第二次弹劾指控可能持续数月的审判结果可能决定杜特尔特是否会被禁止竞选总统。 2028 年大选。只有在两极分化的 24 个席位的参议院中，只有三分之二的人做出有罪判决，才能剥夺她的副总统职位，并永久禁止她当选。杜特尔特仍然是 2028 年总统竞选的领跑者，5 月底的一项调查显示，51% 的受访者计划投票给她。半岛电视台的巴纳比·洛 (Barnaby Lo) 从马尼拉报道，马科斯执政不到两年，众议院 — — 当时由一名总统的表弟开始调查副总统涉嫌腐败。“调查如滚雪球般发展成第一个弹劾申诉……该申诉被最高法院宣告无效，”他说。 “但现在，杜特尔特面临第二次弹劾指控。”杜特尔特和马科斯是该国两个最强大政治王朝的继承人。2022年，他们联合赢得了选举，但他们的联盟此后在日益激烈的不和中瓦解，去年罗德里戈·杜特尔特因与其所谓的“反毒战争”有关的谋杀指控被捕并被移交国际刑事法院（ICC）羁押后，这一联盟进一步恶化。激烈的斗争已经蔓延到参议院，引发了对审判将如何展开的疑问。周一发生了戏剧性的转折，杜特尔特的盟友参议员罗丹特·马科莱塔在审判开始前不久因掠夺罪被捕，使参议院对副总统的支持受到质疑。5月，正当参议院准备收到众议院的弹劾申诉时，杜特尔特的盟友参议员罗纳德·“巴托”·德拉·罗莎在被指控后出现在会议厅。自 11 月以来一直缺席公众视野，并投出了决定性的一票，任命艾伦·彼得·卡耶塔诺 (Alan Peter Cayetano) 为参议院议长。德拉·罗莎 (Dela Rosa) 随后躲藏在参议院，并于 5 月 14 日凌晨溜走，而此时议会大楼内爆发了混乱和枪声。他的下落不明。卡耶塔诺是杜特尔特父亲在 2016 年选举中的竞选搭档。德拉·罗莎也面临国际刑事法院的指控，她在缉毒行动的大部分时间里担任国家警察局长，数千名嫌疑人被杀。他和罗德里戈·杜特尔特均否认有不当行为。大约一个月后，与敌对集团结盟的参议员获得了足够的支持，选举参议员张侨伟为参议院议长。

articles.title: Egg producers settle with DOJ, states over price-fixing complaint
articles.summary: Seventeen states and the Department of Justice have settled a case of alleged price-fixing by major egg producers. Egg prices soared in recent years but have since fallen sharply.
articles.titleZh: 鸡蛋生产商与司法部和各州就价格操纵投诉达成和解
articles.summaryZh: 十七个州和司法部已经就主要鸡蛋生产商涉嫌操纵价格的案件达成和解。近年来鸡蛋价格飙升，但此后急剧下跌。

contentParagraphs.en: Egg producers settle with DOJ, states over price-fixing complaint
contentParagraphs.zh: 鸡蛋生产商与司法部和各州就价格操纵投诉达成和解

contentParagraphs.en: Seventeen states and the Department of Justice have settled a case of alleged price-fixing by major egg producers. Egg prices soared in recent years but have since fallen sharply.
contentParagraphs.zh: 十七个州和司法部已经就主要鸡蛋生产商涉嫌操纵价格的案件达成和解。近年来鸡蛋价格飙升，但此后急剧下跌。

contentParagraphs.en: July 5, 20268:04 AM ET
contentParagraphs.zh: 东部时间 20268 年 7 月 5 日上午 04 点

contentParagraphs.en: Egg producers settle with DOJ, states over price-fixing complaint
contentParagraphs.zh: 鸡蛋生产商与司法部和各州就价格操纵投诉达成和解

contentParagraphs.en: <iframe src="https://www.npr.org/player/embed/nx-s1-5876239-e1/nx-s1-9836743" width="100%" height="290" frameborder="0" scrolling="no" title="NPR embedded audio player">
contentParagraphs.zh: <iframe src =“https://www.npr.org/player/embed/nx-s1-5876239-e1/nx-s1-9836743”width =“100％”height =“290”frameborder =“0”scrolling =“no”title =“NPR嵌入式音频播放器”>

contentParagraphs.en: Seventeen states and the Department of Justice have settled a case of alleged price-fixing by major egg producers. Egg prices soared in recent years but have since fallen sharply.
contentParagraphs.zh: 十七个州和司法部已经就主要鸡蛋生产商涉嫌操纵价格的案件达成和解。近年来鸡蛋价格飙升，但此后急剧下跌。

contentParagraphs.en: AYESHA RASCOE, HOST: You might recall that the price of eggs peaked last year. Now some of the country's biggest egg producers have settled a complaint that they artificially raised prices in recent years. As part of the deal with the Justice Department and more than a dozen states, the companies agreed to donate more than 50 million eggs to food banks around the country. They'll also pay more than $3 million. Prosecutors touted the settlement, but critics say it's not all it's cracked up to be. NPR's Scott Horsley reports.SCOTT HORSLEY, BYLINE: Consumers squawked in recent years when egg prices went through the roof. The major driver was bird flu, which wiped out tens of millions of egg-laying hens. But the Justice Department and attorneys general in 17 states say that's not the whole story. They argue that a handful of major egg producers deliberately pushed prices even higher by making exaggerated bids to a market clearinghouse called Urner Barry. Companies like Urner Barry that collect and publish food prices have come under scrutiny in recent years. Livestock economist David Anderson of Texas A&M University says transparent price information is crucial to making markets work, but it also has the potential to be manipulated.DAVID ANDERSON: There has been an argument that - does that data signal to other players in the market what their competitors are doing, and then can that be used as a way to collude?HORSLEY: Retail egg prices peaked in March of last year, and since then, they've tumbled more than 40%. That price drop is partly the result of a mild flu season, which allowed the flock of egg-laying chickens to rebound. But Angela Huffman, who heads a watchdog group called Farm Action, notes that last March is also when the government put egg producers on notice. Their pricing is under scrutiny.ANGELA HUFFMAN: Once they filed an investigation, prices dropped. So we suspect that these companies kind of ran back home with their tail between their legs, you know, and realized they couldn't get away with it any longer.HORSLEY: Still, Huffman's disappointed. She notes the $3.3 million payment in the settlement is a tiny fraction of the egg company's profits during this period and not likely to be much of a deterrent.HUFFMAN: This isn't making it right for consumers. You know, what are we getting back?HORSLEY: The egg producers - Cal-Maine Foods, Hickman's Egg Ranch and Versova - did not admit to any wrongdoing. Cal-Maine says it did its best to keep supermarkets stocked with eggs during a difficult time. Economist Anderson says there's now such a glut of eggs on the market, many farmers are losing money.ANDERSON: We've had weeks with wholesale egg prices that are probably below egg producers' production costs. But, you know, markets oftentimes overreact both up and down, trying to respond to these signals.HORSLEY: Anderson says if egg producers are fixing prices, they're not very good at it.Scott Horsley, NPR News, Washington.
contentParagraphs.zh: AYESHA RASCOE（主持人）：您可能还记得去年鸡蛋价格达到顶峰。现在，该国一些最大的鸡蛋生产商已经解决了近年来人为提高价格的投诉。作为与司法部和十几个州达成的协议的一部分，这些公司同意向全国各地的食品银行捐赠超过 5000 万个鸡蛋。他们还将支付超过 300 万美元。检察官宣传了这一和解协议，但批评人士表示，这并不像人们所吹捧的那样。 NPR 的斯科特·霍斯利 (Scott Horsley) 报道。 斯科特·霍斯利连线：近年来，当鸡蛋价格飞涨时，消费者抱怨不已。主要驱动因素是禽流感，它杀死了数千万只产蛋母鸡。但司法部和 17 个州的总检察长表示，这并不是故事的全部。他们认为，少数主要鸡蛋生产商通过向名为 Urner Barry 的市场清算所提出夸大的出价，故意推高价格。像 Urner Barry 这样收集和发布食品价格的公司近年来受到了密切关注。德克萨斯 A&M 大学的牲畜经济学家戴维·安德森 (David Anderson) 表示，透明的价格信息对于市场正常运转至关重要，但它也有可能被操纵。 戴维·安德森 (DAVID ANDERSON)：有一种观点认为，这些数据是否向市场上的其他参与者表明其竞争对手正在做什么，然后可以将其用作串通的一种方式吗？ 霍斯利：零售鸡蛋价格在去年 3 月达到顶峰，从那时起，价格已下跌了 40% 以上。价格下跌的部分原因是温和的流感季节，这使得产蛋鸡群数量反弹。但“农场行动”监督组织的负责人安吉拉·霍夫曼指出，去年三月也是政府对鸡蛋生产商发出警告的时候。他们的定价正在接受审查。 安吉拉·霍夫曼：一旦他们提起调查，价格就下降了。所以我们怀疑这些公司夹着尾巴跑回家了，你知道，并意识到他们再也无法逃脱惩罚了。 霍斯利：不过，霍夫曼还是很失望。她指出，和解协议中支付的 330 万美元仅占该鸡蛋公司在此期间利润的一小部分，不太可能产生太大的威慑力。 赫夫曼：这并不适合消费者。你知道，我们能得到什么回报吗？霍斯利：鸡蛋生产商——卡尔-缅因食品公司、希克曼鸡蛋牧场和 Versova——没有承认有任何不当行为。加州缅因州表示，在困难时期，他们已尽力让超市备有鸡蛋。经济学家安德森表示，现在市场上的鸡蛋供过于求，许多农民都在赔钱。安德森：几周来，鸡蛋批发价格可能低于鸡蛋生产商的生产成本。但是，你知道，市场常常会过度反应，无论是向上还是向下，试图对这些信号做出反应。霍斯利：安德森说，如果鸡蛋生产商固定价格，他们就不太擅长。斯科特·霍斯利，NPR 新闻，华盛顿。
