articles.title: These Medicare beneficiaries thought their drug plan was free. Then they lost it
articles.summary: Thousands of people lost coverage over as little as $8 in delinquent payments. They didn't know their zero-dollar premiums had gone up and they owed money. Most now can't get coverage  until 2027.
articles.titleZh: 这些医疗保险受益人认为他们的药物计划是免费的。然后他们就失去了它
articles.summaryZh: 数千人因拖欠 8 美元的款项而失去保险。 他们不知道他们的零美元保费已经上涨，而且他们欠了钱。现在大多数人要到 2027 年才能获得保险。

contentParagraphs.en: Thousands of Medicare beneficiaries lost drug coverage after their premiums went up and they didn't know.
contentParagraphs.zh: 数以千计的医疗保险受益人在保费上涨后失去了药物保险，而他们却不知道。

contentParagraphs.en: fstop123/iStockphoto/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: fstop123/iStockphoto/Getty Images

contentParagraphs.en: fstop123/iStockphoto/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: fstop123/iStockphoto/Getty Images

contentParagraphs.en: Stay up to date with our Up First newsletter, sent every weekday morning.
contentParagraphs.zh: 请随时关注我们每个工作日早上发送的 Up First 时事通讯。

contentParagraphs.en: Jude Pare and his partner, Diane Tix, live in rural Minnesota until temperatures dip below freezing, when they take refuge in Arizona for the winter. While away, their mail is forwarded. But Pare, 77, said he didn't receive any warning from his Medicare prescription drug plan that his $0 monthly premium was about to increase.
contentParagraphs.zh: 裘德·帕尔（Jude Pare）和他的伴侣黛安·蒂克斯（Diane Tix）住在明尼苏达州的乡村，直到气温降至冰点以下，他们才到亚利桑那州避难过冬。当他们离开时，他们的邮件会被转发。但 77 岁的 Pare 表示，他的 Medicare 处方药计划没有收到任何关于他每月 0 美元保费即将增加的警告。

contentParagraphs.en: So he didn't know he had a bill to pay. After he and Tix returned home to Minnesota in April, they got a letter from Wellcare, the insurer that provided his drug plan, saying his coverage had been terminated after three months of unpaid premiums totaling $28.80. Under Medicare's rules, he can't enroll in a plan again until the fall, for coverage beginning in 2027.
contentParagraphs.zh: 所以他不知道他有账单要付。 4 月份，他和蒂克斯回到明尼苏达州的家后，他们收到了为他提供药物计划的保险公司 Wellcare 的一封信，信中称他的保险在三个月未付保费总计 28.80 美元后被终止。根据 Medicare 的规定，他在秋季之前不能再次参加 2027 年开始的保险计划。

contentParagraphs.en: Pare takes Xarelto, a blood thinner that reduces his risk of strokes, blood clots, and pulmonary embolism. "He could bleed to death without it," Tix said. A 90-day supply of the drug costs about $1,800 using a coupon from GoodRx, a discount drug website, she said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 帕雷服用了 Xarelto，一种血液稀释剂，可以降低中风、血栓和肺栓塞的风险。 “如果没有它，他可能会失血过多而死，”蒂克斯说。她说，使用折扣药物网站 GoodRx 的优惠券，90 天的药物供应成本约为 1,800 美元。

contentParagraphs.en: Pare is among tens of thousands of Medicare beneficiaries who were on Wellcare's Value Script drug plan who will likely go without prescription drug coverage for the rest of the year because they didn't pay premiums for three months.
contentParagraphs.zh: Pare 是参加 Wellcare Value Script 药物计划的数万名医疗保险受益人之一，他们可能在今年剩余时间里没有处方药保险，因为他们已经三个月没有支付保费了。

contentParagraphs.en: Next year, thousands more people in 32 states and Washington, D.C., enrolled in zero-premium drug plans from Wellcare and other insurance companies may find themselves in the same situation if their premiums go up and they don't realize it, according to a KFF Health News analysis of drug plan data. Premiums and other changes for 2027 will be unveiled in September.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据 KFF 健康新闻对药物计划数据的分析，明年，32 个州和华盛顿特区的数千人参加了 Wellcare 和其他保险公司的零保费药物计划，如果他们的保费上涨而他们没有意识到，他们可能会发现自己处于同样的情况。 2027 年的保费和其他变化将于 9 月公布。

contentParagraphs.en: Caught off guard
contentParagraphs.zh: 措手不及

contentParagraphs.en: Going without medication can be life-threatening, especially for Medicare beneficiaries. Nearly 90% take one or more prescription drugs, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. Almost half live with four or more chronic health conditions that can cause functional or cognitive impairments.
contentParagraphs.zh: 不服药可能会危及生命，特别是对于医疗保险受益人而言。根据美国疾病控制与预防中心的数据，近 90% 的人服用一种或多种处方药。几乎一半的人患有四种或更多可能导致功能或认知障碍的慢性健康状况。

contentParagraphs.en: By the time Jude Pare and his partner, Diane Tix, found out he owed $28.80 for three months of drug plan premiums, his coverage had been canceled. He is among tens of thousands of Medicare beneficiaries who will likely go without prescription drug coverage for the rest of the year after their drug plans were canceled.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当 Jude Pare 和他的伴侣 Diane Tix 发现他欠了 3 个月的药物计划保费 28.80 美元时，他的保险已被取消。他是数以万计的医疗保险受益人之一，他们的药物计划被取消后，今年剩余时间可能没有处方药保险。

contentParagraphs.en: Congress added prescription drug coverage to Medicare in 2003. But the coverage is administered by commercial insurance companies, which compete fiercely with one another for the business of about 56 million Medicare beneficiaries enrolled in drug plans.
contentParagraphs.zh: 国会于 2003 年将处方药保险纳入 Medicare。但该保险由商业保险公司管理，这些保险公司为了争夺加入药物计划的约 5600 万 Medicare 受益人的业务而展开激烈竞争。

contentParagraphs.en: Zero-dollar or very low monthly premiums have helped make Wellcare's Value Script the bestselling stand-alone prescription drug plan in Medicare, with nearly 6 million customers across the U.S., according to government data. But in 26 states and Washington, D.C., some Value Script members who didn't have to pay a premium last year were caught off guard by increases in 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据政府数据，零美元或极低的每月保费使 Wellcare 的 Value Script 成为 Medicare 中最畅销的独立处方药计划，在全美拥有近 600 万客户。但在 26 个州和华盛顿特区，一些去年无需支付保费的 Value Script 会员对 2026 年的涨价感到措手不及。

contentParagraphs.en: After a two-month grace period — which Wellcare extended to three — Medicare drug plans can drop customers who don't pay their premiums, no matter how small the amount. Some members who lost their coverage in Nevada, for example, owed as little as $8.10 for three months, according to a KFF Health News analysis of Medicare drug plan data.
contentParagraphs.zh: 经过两个月的宽限期（Wellcare 将其延长至三个月）后，医疗保险药物计划可以放弃那些不支付保费的客户，无论金额有多小。例如，根据 KFF 健康新闻对 Medicare 药物计划数据的分析，一些在内华达州失去保险的会员三个月的欠款低至 8.10 美元。

contentParagraphs.en: Wellcare terminated coverage for about 140,000 Value Script beneficiaries in April, according to a person with knowledge of the matter who was not authorized to speak publicly about it and feared reprisals at work for doing so. About 40,000 of the people who were dropped may be able to enroll in new coverage immediately because they have low incomes and receive financial assistance through a program Medicare calls "Extra Help."
contentParagraphs.zh: 据一位知情人士透露，Wellcare 在 4 月份终止了约 140,000 名 Value Script 受益人的保险，该人士未获授权公开谈论此事，并担心这样做会在工作中遭到报复。大约 40,000 名被剔除的人可能能够立即加入新的保险，因为他们收入较低，并通过 Medicare 称为“额外帮助”的计划获得经济援助。

contentParagraphs.en: Multiple state officials said they had heard the same disenrollment figures, including Nevada's insurance commissioner, Ned Gaines, who chairs the National Association of Insurance Commissioners' senior issues task force; Rebecca Gouty, director of the State Health Insurance Assistance Program in West Virginia; and Tim Smolen, director of Washington state's Statewide Health Insurance Benefits Advisors.
contentParagraphs.zh: 多位州官员表示，他们也听到了同样的退出数据，其中包括内华达州保险专员内德·盖恩斯 (Ned Gaines)，他是全国保险专员协会高级问题工作组的主席； Rebecca Gouty，西弗吉尼亚州国家健康保险援助计划主任；以及华盛顿州全州健康保险福利顾问主任蒂姆·斯莫伦（Tim Smolen）。

contentParagraphs.en: The West Virginia and Washington initiatives are part of the federally funded State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP), which provides free, unbiased help navigating Medicare.
contentParagraphs.zh: 西弗吉尼亚州和华盛顿州的举措是联邦政府资助的州健康保险援助计划 (SHIP) 的一部分，该计划提供免费、公正的医疗保险帮助。

contentParagraphs.en: The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, which oversees Medicare drug plans, declined to provide the number of Value Script members who lost coverage due to unpaid premiums. "The agency does not publicly provide plan-specific disenrollment figures or state-level breakdowns related to the non-payment of premiums," Christopher Krepich, a spokesperson, said in a written statement to KFF Health News.
contentParagraphs.zh: 负责监督医疗保险药物计划的医疗保险和医疗补助服务中心拒绝提供因未付保费而失去保险的 Value Script 会员人数。发言人克里斯托弗·克雷皮奇 (Christopher Krepich) 在给 KFF 健康新闻的书面声明中表示：“该机构不会公开提供特定于计划的退出数据或与未缴纳保费相关的州级细目。”

contentParagraphs.en: Centene Corp., Wellcare's parent company, also declined to provide disenrollment numbers.
contentParagraphs.zh: Wellcare 的母公司 Centene Corp. 也拒绝提供退出人数。

contentParagraphs.en: "We recognize how disruptive a loss of coverage can be and are committed to helping members understand their options," said Sarah Baiocchi, senior vice president for specialty and prescription drug plans at Centene. She acknowledged that "some members in our Value Script plan experienced a premium for the first time, or for the first time in several years."
contentParagraphs.zh: Centene 负责专业和处方药计划的高级副总裁 Sarah Baiocchi 表示：“我们认识到失去承保范围可能会造成多大的破坏，并致力于帮助会员了解他们的选择。”她承认，“我们的 Value Script 计划中的一些会员第一次或几年来第一次经历了溢价。”

contentParagraphs.en: Baiocchi said all Value Script members received a CMS-required annual notice of changes in September, before the premium increases took effect.
contentParagraphs.zh: Baiocchi 表示，在保费上涨生效之前，所有 Value Script 会员都在 9 月份收到了 CMS 要求的年度变更通知。

contentParagraphs.en: A version of the booklet sent to members in two states and Washington, D.C., is 21 pages long. The new premium is mentioned on pages 3 and 8, along with changes to out-of-pocket costs and how to find updates on covered drugs and network pharmacies.
contentParagraphs.zh: 发送给两个州和华盛顿特区会员的小册子版本长达 21 页。第 3 页和第 8 页提到了新的保费，以及自付费用的变化以及如何查找承保药物和网络药房的更新。

contentParagraphs.en: Wayne Bennett lost his Medicare drug coverage because he didn't pay the premium, which was free last year but — without his knowing — went up this year. "Medicare should be doing s
contentParagraphs.zh: 韦恩·贝内特 (Wayne Bennett) 失去了医疗保险药物承保，因为他没有支付保费，去年保费是免费的，但在他不知情的情况下，今年保费上涨了。 “医疗保险应该做

articles.title: Hate food waste? 7 creative ways to turn your leftovers into a new meal
articles.summary: We asked our audience to share their favorite go-to recipes for leftovers. Here are seven dishes — like stuffed peppers and a biryani casserole — that can help you use up all your fridge scraps.
articles.titleZh: 讨厌食物浪费？将剩菜变成新餐的 7 种创意方法
articles.summaryZh: 我们要求观众分享他们最喜欢的剩菜食谱。这里有七道菜——比如酿辣椒和印度比尔亚尼菜砂锅——可以帮助你用完冰箱里的所有剩菜。

contentParagraphs.en: Turn the last few ingredients and leftovers in your fridge into completely new dishes you can enjoy
contentParagraphs.zh: 将冰箱里的最后几种食材和剩菜变成您可以享用的全新菜肴

contentParagraphs.en: kaisersosa67/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: kaisersosa67/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: kaisersosa67/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: kaisersosa67/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Leftovers are a strangely controversial topic. Some people love them (eating cold pizza while standing in front of the fridge is a treasured pastime of mine). And some people can't stand the idea of eating yesterday's food … again!
contentParagraphs.zh: 剩菜剩饭是一个奇怪的有争议的话题。有些人喜欢它们（站在冰箱前吃冷披萨是我珍惜的消遣）。有些人无法忍受再次吃昨天的食物！

contentParagraphs.en: Well, it turns out that a few of you in our audience are indeed "leftovers people." And we're not just talking about eating Monday's casserole on Thursday or having that last bit of takeout chicken parm.
contentParagraphs.zh: 嗯，事实证明，我们观众中有几个确实是“剩女”。我们谈论的不仅仅是周四吃周一的砂锅菜或吃最后一点外卖鸡肉。

contentParagraphs.en: Some of you enjoy turning them into completely new dishes, stuffing them into ancho peppers or making Polish beet soup just to use up your fridge's odds and ends.
contentParagraphs.zh: 有些人喜欢把它们变成全新的菜肴，把它们塞进安乔辣椒里，或者制作波兰甜菜汤，只是为了用完冰箱里的零碎东西。

contentParagraphs.en: Here are seven ways to transform your leftovers, shared by our audience. They come from a question we asked last month in a story and podcast episode on how to reduce food waste, by journalist Emily Siner. These have been edited for length and clarity.
contentParagraphs.zh: 我们的观众分享了以下七种改变剩菜剩饭的方法。它们来自我们上个月在记者艾米丽·西纳（Emily Siner）关于如何减少食物浪费的故事和播客节目中提出的一个问题。为了篇幅和清晰度，这些内容已经过编辑。

contentParagraphs.en: Layer your leftovers in a biryani casserole
contentParagraphs.zh: 将剩菜放入 Biryani 砂锅中

contentParagraphs.en: Whenever I have random bits of meat, veggie stir-fry or dal, I layer them into a baking dish, alternating with rice (leftover rice works pretty well for this). To elevate it, I throw fresh mint on top and drizzle over a little warm milk infused with saffron strands. Seal it tightly with foil, bake at 400 F, and it tastes brand new. — Pradosh Mohapatra
contentParagraphs.zh: 每当我有随机的肉、蔬菜炒菜或木豆时，我都会将它们分层放入烤盘中，与米饭交替放置（剩下的米饭非常适合这样做）。为了提升它，我在上面撒上新鲜薄荷，然后淋上一点加入藏红花丝的温牛奶。用箔纸紧紧密封，在 400 F 下烘烤，味道焕然一新。 — 普拉多什·莫哈帕特拉

contentParagraphs.en: Smash your leftovers into a crunchy panini
contentParagraphs.zh: 将剩菜捣碎成松脆的帕尼尼

contentParagraphs.en: Grab your favorite bread and slather it with an easy DIY aioli (whisk together mayo, olive oil, sriracha, garlic or garlic powder, lemon juice and Dijon mustard). Stuff it with your leftover meats and veggies, then hit it in the sandwich press until it's hot and crunchy. — Pradosh Mohapatra
contentParagraphs.zh: 拿起你最喜欢的面包，涂上一层简单的自制蒜泥蛋黄酱（将蛋黄酱、橄榄油、拉差辣椒酱、大蒜或大蒜粉、柠檬汁和第戎芥末搅拌在一起）。把剩下的肉和蔬菜塞进去，然后放入三明治压榨机中，直到变热变脆。 — 普拉多什·莫哈帕特拉

contentParagraphs.en: Don't sleep on that chicken carcass
contentParagraphs.zh: 别睡在鸡尸体上

contentParagraphs.en: The one thing I look for the most is a leftover rotisserie chicken. After thanking the chicken for her service, I separate any usable meat and refrigerate. The rest goes into a stock pot with leftover frozen celery tops, onion skins and maybe the odd garlic clove. I add in bay leaf, sage powder, salt and ground pepper. After it cooks for hours, I'll strain [the stock], taste it for flavor, and then decide what to do with it.
contentParagraphs.zh: 我最寻找的一件事是剩下的烤鸡。感谢鸡的服务后，我将所有可用的肉分开并冷藏。剩下的放入汤锅中，里面有剩余的冷冻芹菜顶、洋葱皮，也许还有奇怪的蒜瓣。我加入月桂叶、鼠尾草粉、盐和胡椒粉。煮了几个小时后，我会过滤[汤]，品尝它的味道，然后决定如何处理它。

contentParagraphs.en: One of my favorite ways to use the leftover chicken and stock is to make chicken and dumplings. Another is a porridge-like soup, kind of like a juk. I put a quarter of a cup of rice, diced up vegetables and leftover chicken in 32 oz. or so of stock. Good for breakfast, lunch or dinner. — Jennifer Webster
contentParagraphs.zh: 我最喜欢使用剩下的鸡肉和高汤的方法之一是制作鸡肉和饺子。另一种是类似粥的汤，有点像juk。我把四分之一杯米饭、蔬菜丁和剩下的鸡肉放入 32 盎司中。左右的库存。适合早餐、午餐或晚餐。 — 詹妮弗·韦伯斯特

contentParagraphs.en: Stuff your leftovers into ancho peppers
contentParagraphs.zh: 把剩菜塞进凤尾椒里

contentParagraphs.en: Slice ancho peppers lengthwise into halves, de-seed them and steam with a little water in the microwave. Then stuff them with any combo of roasted veggies like onion, squash, broccoli and carrots, and rice or beans.
contentParagraphs.zh: 将凤尾椒纵向切成两半，去籽，用微波炉加少许水蒸。然后在里面塞上任何烤蔬菜的组合，比如洋葱、南瓜、西兰花和胡萝卜，以及米饭或豆类。

contentParagraphs.en: Make a sauce of tomatoes or tomato paste and leftover chopped herbs or dried spices like cumin, chili powder, smoked paprika and oregano. Spread a thin layer of sauce in a dish, then add the [stuffed] peppers, the rest of the sauce on top, and cover with foil. Bake at 350 F for 20 minutes. Top with chopped cilantro if you have it. — Jennifer Sabie
contentParagraphs.zh: 用番茄或番茄酱以及剩余的切碎香草或干香料（如小茴香、辣椒粉、烟熏辣椒粉和牛至）制作酱汁。在盘子里涂上一层薄薄的酱汁，然后加入[酿]辣椒，把剩下的酱汁放在上面，然后盖上箔纸。在 350 F 下烘烤 20 分钟。如果有的话，在上面撒上切碎的香菜。 — 詹妮弗·萨比

contentParagraphs.en: Scramble your leftovers with tofu for brekkie
contentParagraphs.zh: 将剩菜和豆腐炒成早餐

contentParagraphs.en: Chop leftover veggies and add them to tofu for a high-protein low-fat breakfast. To prep the tofu, squeeze out excess water then cube or crumble it.
contentParagraphs.zh: 把剩下的蔬菜切碎，加入豆腐中，就是一顿高蛋白低脂肪的早餐。准备豆腐时，挤出多余的水，然后切成方块或粉碎。

contentParagraphs.en: Heat a teaspoon of neutral oil in a skillet over low-medium heat. Then toss the tofu with nutritional yeast and brown slightly. Add chopped veggies like peas, carrots, potatoes, green beans, limas, etc.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在平底锅中用中低火加热一茶匙中性油。然后将豆腐与营养酵母一起搅拌，稍微煎成褐色。添加切碎的蔬菜，如豌豆、胡萝卜、土豆、青豆、青豆等。

contentParagraphs.en: I also add seeds like sesame, poppy and raw pumpkin seeds; and herbs and spices like sage, turmeric, rosemary, thyme, dried onion or garlic or salt-free blend. Simmer until flavors blended.
contentParagraphs.zh: 我还添加了芝麻、罂粟和生南瓜子等种子；以及香草和香料，如鼠尾草、姜黄、迷迭香、百里香、干洋葱或大蒜或无盐混合物。煮至味道混合。

contentParagraphs.en: If you have one or two pieces of fruit, slice and serve alongside for a beautiful and tasty plate. — Jennifer Sabie
contentParagraphs.zh: 如果您有一两片水果，请将其切片并与一起食用，形成美丽可口的盘子。 — 詹妮弗·萨比

contentParagraphs.en: Make a Polish beet soup to use up your odds and ends
contentParagraphs.zh: 做一份波兰甜菜汤来耗尽你的零碎东西

contentParagraphs.en: Barszcz Ukraiński, a sweet and sour beet soup with chunky vegetables, can really use up a lot of odds and ends in the fridge. As long as you have the requisite beets, you can add beans, a variety of vegetables and meat if you like. Then add vinegar and sugar to taste.
contentParagraphs.zh: Barszcz Ukraiński 是一种糖醋甜菜汤，里面有大块的蔬菜，真的可以用掉冰箱里的很多杂物。只要有必要的甜菜，如果你愿意，你可以添加豆类、各种蔬菜和肉类。然后加入醋和糖调味。

contentParagraphs.en: Toppings include leftover boiled or mashed potatoes, hard boiled eggs, sour cream or yogurt, chopped herbs (classically dill), meat (classically kielbasa, but use what you like), horseradish, or spicy mustard. Toss the toppings in the sweet-sour soup and enjoy. — Courtney Zola
contentParagraphs.zh: 配料包括剩下的煮土豆或土豆泥、煮鸡蛋、酸奶油或酸奶、切碎的香草（通常是莳萝）、肉（通常是波兰熏肠，但可以使用你喜欢的东西）、辣根或辣芥末。将配料放入糖醋汤中即可享用。 — 考特尼·佐拉

contentParagraphs.en: Press your leftovers into a cheesy quesadilla
contentParagraphs.zh: 将剩菜压入奶酪玉米饼中

contentParagraphs.en: Flour tortillas stay great forever in the fridge and are always looking for a way to get into trouble. Add any combo of meat, like shrimp, and veggies, like charred onions or mushrooms, with shredded cheese. — Paula Stellefson
contentParagraphs.zh: 面粉玉米饼在冰箱里永远保存完好，而且总是在寻找陷入麻烦的方法。添加任何肉类（如虾）和蔬菜（如烧焦的洋葱或蘑菇）和切碎的奶酪的组合。 — 保拉·斯特莱夫森

contentParagraphs.en: The story was edited by Meghan Keane. The visual editor is CJ Riculan. We'd love to hear from you. Leave us a voicemail at 202-216-9823, or email us at LifeKit@npr.org.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这个故事由梅根·基恩编辑。视觉编辑是 CJ Riculan。我们很乐意听取您的意见。请致电 202-216-9823 给我们留言，或发送电子邮件至 LifeKit@npr.org。

contentParagraphs.en: Listen to Life Kit on Apple Podcasts and Spotify, and sign up for our newsletter. Follow us on Instagram: @nprlifekit.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在 Apple 播客和 Spotify 上收听 Life Kit，并订阅我们的时事通讯。在 Instagram 上关注我们：@nprlifekit。

articles.title: Marriage used to be a glide path to citizenship. Now there are more speed bumps
articles.summary: Spouses of U.S. citizens have traditionally had a special place in immigration law. That's no longer the case, according to the administration and immigration lawyers.
articles.titleZh: 婚姻曾经是获得公民身份的捷径。现在有更多的减速带
articles.summaryZh: 传统上，美国公民的配偶在移民法中占有特殊地位。政府和移民律师表示，情况已不再如此。

contentParagraphs.en: People wave U.S. flags to celebrate becoming U.S. citizens after taking the oath of allegiance during a naturalization ceremony in Boston in January 2025.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2025 年 1 月，人们在波士顿举行的入籍仪式上宣誓效忠后，挥舞美国国旗庆祝成为美国公民。

contentParagraphs.en: Stay up to date with our Politics newsletter, sent weekly.
contentParagraphs.zh: 随时了解我们每周发送的政治时事通讯。

contentParagraphs.en: The Trump administration's sweeping effort to slow down the rate of legal migration has affected a group traditionally immune from such efforts: spouses of U.S. citizens.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普政府为减缓合法移民速度而采取的全面措施影响了传统上不受此类措施影响的群体：美国公民的配偶。

contentParagraphs.en: The administration has implemented a slew of policy changes since President Trump returned to the White House last year, ranging from pausing immigrant visas for people from 75 countries to imposing greater scrutiny of applicants at green-card interviews and widening the scope of who is a target for deportation. The changes have hit all immigrants hard, including those who sought to enter and stay in the country through marriage.
contentParagraphs.zh: 自特朗普总统去年重返白宫以来，政府实施了一系列政策变化，从暂停向来自 75 个国家的人发放移民签证，到在绿卡面试时对申请人进行更严格的审查，以及扩大驱逐目标的范围。这些变化对所有移民都造成了沉重打击，包括那些寻求通过婚姻进入并留在该国的移民。

contentParagraphs.en: Some non-U.S.-citizen spouses have been separated from their American loved ones and are afraid to engage with the U.S. immigration system, according to lawyers and NPR interviews with affected families.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据律师和美国国家公共广播电台对受影响家庭的采访，一些非美国公民的配偶已经与他们的美国亲人分离，并且害怕参与美国移民系统。

contentParagraphs.en: "Life has become a lot more difficult for Americans who are married to somebody who is not born in this country," said Ashley DeAzevedo, executive director of American Families United. The organization advocates for U.S. citizen spouses and immediate family members of those engaged in various immigration processes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国家庭联合会执行董事阿什利·德阿泽维多表示：“对于与非出生在这个国家的人结婚的美国人来说，生活变得更加困难。”该组织为参与各种移民程序的美国公民配偶和直系亲属提供支持。

contentParagraphs.en: The organization's membership has grown over the last year as more people are affected by the rapid policy changes, she said. Now there are about 1.4 million people seeking the group's support in the U.S., and about 300,000 outside the country — made up of people who have left the U.S., as well as those who want to come in.
contentParagraphs.zh: 她说，随着越来越多的人受到快速政策变化的影响，该组织的成员数量在去年有所增加。现在，美国境内约有 140 万人寻求该组织的支持，国外约有 30 万人寻求该组织的支持，其中包括离开美国的人和想入境美国的人。

contentParagraphs.en: "We saw so many of our members make the decision to self-deport, to leave the country for fear of this indefinite detention," DeAzevedo said. "We saw some members who had their spouses detained — and that was something we had not experienced previously because there was always this prioritization of who was going to be detained."
contentParagraphs.zh: 德阿泽维多说：“我们看到我们的许多成员因担心无限期拘留而做出自我驱逐的决定，离开这个国家。” “我们看到一些成员的配偶被拘留——这是我们以前从未经历过的事情，因为总是有优先考虑谁将被拘留的情况。”

contentParagraphs.en: Sharvari Dalal-Dheini, senior director of government relations at the American Immigration Lawyers Association, said the United States government has always vetted and scrutinized immigrants who sought to stay in the U.S. through marriage.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国移民律师协会政府关系高级总监沙瓦里·达拉尔-戴尼表示，美国政府一直对通过婚姻寻求留在美国的移民进行审查和审查。

contentParagraphs.en: However, while applying to stay, spouses of U.S. citizens were generally not swept up in broader immigration enforcement efforts.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然而，在申请居留时，美国公民的配偶通常不会被卷入更广泛的移民执法行动中。

contentParagraphs.en: "This group of individuals have always had a special place under the law," Dalal-Dheini said. "Spouses of U.S. citizens aren't subject to the immigrant quotas. They don't have to have a cap. Spouses of U.S. citizens don't [have to have maintained] their legal status here in order to adjust. And so the law has considered them to be a privileged class.
contentParagraphs.zh: 达拉勒-戴尼说：“根据法律，这一群体一直享有特殊地位。” “美国公民的配偶不受移民配额的限制。他们不必有上限。美国公民的配偶不必[必须保持]他们在这里的合法身份才能适应。因此法律认为他们是特权阶层。

contentParagraphs.en: "But this administration is treating them like all other immigrants."
contentParagraphs.zh: “但本届政府像对待所有其他移民一样对待他们。”

contentParagraphs.en: The administration says prior presidents should also have scrutinized such marriage-related applications more closely, and U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) is just complying with the law.
contentParagraphs.zh: 政府表示，前任总统也应该更仔细地审查此类与婚姻相关的申请，而美国公民及移民服务局（USCIS）只是遵守法律。

contentParagraphs.en: In a statement to NPR, USCIS spokesman Zach Kahler said verifying identities and personal histories of all people seeking immigration benefits, like a green card or citizenship, requires a rigorous process — "one that prioritizes the safety of the American people by more thoroughly screening and vetting all aliens."
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国公民及移民服务局发言人扎克·卡勒在给美国公共广播电台的一份声明中表示，核实所有寻求绿卡或公民身份等移民福利的人的身份和个人历史需要严格的程序——“通过更彻底地筛查和审查所有外国人来优先考虑美国人民的安全。”

contentParagraphs.en: He said just marrying a U.S. citizen and beginning the petition process does not protect someone from deportation.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他说，仅仅与美国公民结婚并开始申请程序并不能保护某人免遭驱逐出境。

contentParagraphs.en: "A pending or approved Form I-130, Petition for Alien Relative, does not confer any immigration status. All aliens are expected to comply with U.S. immigration laws," Kahler said. "Those who entered without inspection or who remain in the United States beyond their permitted stay are illegal aliens who may be subject to immigration enforcement action."
contentParagraphs.zh: “待决或批准的 I-130 表格（外籍亲属申请表）并不授予任何移民身份。所有外国人都应遵守美国移民法，”卡勒说。 “那些未经检查入境或超出允许停留时间留在美国的人是非法外国人，可能会受到移民执法行动。”

contentParagraphs.en: Family and fiancé petitions make up nearly half of green-card approvals
contentParagraphs.zh: 家庭和未婚夫的申请占绿卡批准的近一半

contentParagraphs.en: Immediate family sponsorship, including for spouses and fiancés, is one of the top ways U.S. citizens interact with the immigration system.
contentParagraphs.zh: 直系亲属担保，包括配偶和未婚夫的担保，是美国公民与移民系统互动的主要方式之一。

contentParagraphs.en: The latest data released by the Homeland Security Department, from 2024, shows that about 343,000 people received their green cards through their spouses — about a quarter of all green-card approvals. For about a decade, the number has hovered somewhere between 200,000 and 340,000 people.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国国土安全部发布的 2024 年以来的最新数据显示，约 343,000 人通过配偶获得绿卡——约占绿卡批准总数的四分之一。大约十年来，这个数字一直徘徊在 20 万到 34 万之间。

contentParagraphs.en: The number of approved green cards doubles when accounting for other immediate family members who can provide sponsorship for immigrants, like children and parents. That suggests how important this pathway is for U.S. citizens and their immigrant family members.
contentParagraphs.zh: 如果算上可以为移民提供担保的其他直系亲属，例如孩子和父母，则批准的绿卡数量会增加一倍。这表明这条途径对于美国公民及其移民家庭成员有多么重要。

contentParagraphs.en: The average processing time for each petition was 13 months for family members, and seven months for fiancés — largely in line with wait times from early 2025, before Trump's policies took effect.
contentParagraphs.zh: 每份请愿书的平均处理时间（家庭成员）为 13 个月，未婚夫（未婚夫）为 7 个月——基本上与 2025 年初特朗普政策生效前的等待时间一致。

contentParagraphs.en: In the first quarter of the 2026 fiscal year, 167,401 immediate family relative petitions were approved, and 8,612 fiancé petitions were approved.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026财年第一季度，有167,401份直系亲属申请获得批准，8,612份未婚夫申请获得批准。

contentParagraphs.en: The number of petitions approved has generally increased and decreased across administrations.
contentParagraphs.zh: 各主管部门批准的请愿书数量普遍有所增加或减少。

contentParagraphs.en: Chaos for U.S. citizens, including military members
contentParagraphs.zh: 包括军人在内的美国公民陷入混乱

contentParagraphs.en: The data may mask other ways noncitizen spouses could be affected. That's particularly true for those from one of over 70 countries that face holds for a wide range of travel and immigrant visas.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这些数据可能掩盖了非公民配偶可能受到影响的其他方式。对于来自 70 多个国家之一的人来说尤其如此，这些国家面临着各种旅行和移民签证的搁置。

contentParagraphs.en: One such case is Es', a green-card holder married to a U.S. citizen. She was born in one of the 39 countries subject to a travel ban to the U.S., which was implemented last year. The pause means that although she has been in the country for three decades, her application for citizenship filed last year has not yet been reviewed.
contentParagraphs.zh: Es 就是这样一个案例，她是绿卡持有者，与美国公民结婚。她出生于去年实施的美国旅行禁令的 39 个国家之一。这一暂停意味着，尽管她已经在该国生活了三十年，但她去年提交的公民身份申请尚未得到审查。

contentParagraphs.en: There is no exception to the travel ban, even for spouses of U.S. military members.
contentParagraphs.zh: 旅行禁令也不例外，即使是美国军人的配偶也是如此。

contentParagraphs.en: The last few months have thrown the couples' lives into chaos.
contentParagraphs.zh: 过去几个月让这对夫妇的生活陷入混乱。

contentParagraphs.en: "We are due to [move] to Germany," Es said, speaking on the condition of anonymity because her husband is in the Army and her immigration case is pendin
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们将[搬]到德国，”埃斯说，她要求匿名，因为她的丈夫在军队，而她的移民案正在审理中。

articles.title: Fast walkers in their 80s cut their risk of cognitive decline by half, study finds
articles.summary: A new study of people 80 and older with exceptional gaits finds fast walkers have about a 50% lower risk of cognitive decline, showing the connection between physical health and brain health.
articles.titleZh: 研究发现 80 多岁快走的人认知能力下降的风险降低一半
articles.summaryZh: 一项针对 80 岁及以上步态异常的老年人的新研究发现，快走者认知能力下降的风险降低约 50%，这表明身体健康与大脑健康之间存在联系。

contentParagraphs.en: In a study, researchers found that people in their 80s and older who walked faster than their peers were less likely to develop cognitive decline.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在一项研究中，研究人员发现，80 多岁及以上的人如果比同龄人走得快，出现认知能力下降的可能性较小。

contentParagraphs.en: FG Trade/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: FG 贸易/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: FG Trade/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: FG 贸易/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Crossword puzzles and brain teasers have long been touted as ways to keep the mind sharp. But a new study points to another strategy that may matter just as much: staying fast on your feet.
contentParagraphs.zh: 长期以来，填字游戏和脑筋急转弯一直被认为是保持头脑敏锐的方法。但一项新的研究指出了另一个可能同样重要的策略：保持快速行动。

contentParagraphs.en: Researchers have found that people in their 80s who maintain an exceptionally quick walking pace, dubbed "super movers," are also far more likely to stay mentally sharp compared to their slower-moving peers of the same age.
contentParagraphs.zh: 研究人员发现，与行动缓慢的同龄人相比，保持极快步行速度（被称为“超级步行者”）的 80 多岁的人也更有可能保持思维敏捷。

contentParagraphs.en: "A super mover is someone who is older than age 80 and performing much better than their peers," says Dr. Sofiya Milman of the Albert Einstein College of Medicine, one of the study's authors.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该研究的作者之一、阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦医学院的索菲亚·米尔曼博士说：“超级推动者是指年龄超过 80 岁且表现比同龄人好得多的人。”

contentParagraphs.en: Milman and her collaborators analyzed data from nearly 4,000 older adults enrolled in a long-term aging study. Participants had taken a timed walking test and the fastest 9% – who had a gait speed at least 1.5 standard deviations above the average of their same aged peers – qualified as super movers. Those individuals were also markedly less likely to experience cognitive decline.
contentParagraphs.zh: Milman 和她的合作者分析了参与一项长期衰老研究的近 4,000 名老年人的数据。参与者接受了定时步行测试，最快的 9%（他们的步态速度比同龄同龄人的平均水平至少高出 1.5 个标准差）有资格成为超级运动者。这些人出现认知能力下降的可能性也明显较小。

contentParagraphs.en: "The biggest takeaway was that super movers are about 50% less likely to develop cognitive decline than their peers who are not super movers, which is very impressive," Milman says. The results are published in the medical journal Neurology.
contentParagraphs.zh: 米尔曼说：“最大的收获是，与非超级行动者的同龄人相比，超级行动者出现认知能力下降的可能性要低 50%，这非常令人印象深刻。”研究结果发表在医学杂志《神经病学》上。

contentParagraphs.en: The muscle health connection
contentParagraphs.zh: 肌肉健康的联系

contentParagraphs.en: Walking well requires balance, coordination and strength, all of which depend on healthy muscle, says Bonnie Tsui, a science writer and author of On Muscle: The Stuff That Moves Us and Why It Matters.
contentParagraphs.zh: 科普作家兼《论肌肉：让我们感动的因素及其重要性》一书的作者 Bonnie Tsui 表示，良好的行走需要平衡、协调和力量，而所有这些都取决于健康的肌肉。

contentParagraphs.en: "I think that the finding isn't surprising because we know that muscle health is very much correlated with cognitive health, especially as we age," Tsui says. "Exercise makes your muscles grow, but it also makes your brain grow."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我认为这一发现并不令人惊讶，因为我们知道肌肉健康与认知健康密切相关，尤其是随着年龄的增长，”徐说。 “锻炼可以让你的肌肉生长，但它也可以让你的大脑生长。”

contentParagraphs.en: Prior research has linked regular exercise to greater volume in the hippocampus, the brain's hub for memory and navigation. The new study found that super movers tended to preserve hippocampal volume as they aged.
contentParagraphs.zh: 先前的研究已将定期锻炼与海马体的体积增大联系起来，海马体是大脑的记忆和导航中心。这项新研究发现，随着年龄的增长，超级运动者往往会保持海马体积。

contentParagraphs.en: Want the latest stories on the science of healthy living? Subscribe to NPR's Health newsletter.
contentParagraphs.zh: 想了解健康生活科学的最新故事吗？订阅 NPR 的健康通讯。

contentParagraphs.en: Tsui says the benefits trace back to what happens inside contracting muscles during exercise.
contentParagraphs.zh: Tsui 表示，这些好处可以追溯到运动过程中收缩肌肉内部发生的情况。

contentParagraphs.en: "Muscle is an endocrine tissue, which means when we move, our muscles release signaling molecules that affect other body systems, including boosting brain cell growth and regulating metabolism," she says. "So muscle health is cognitive health."
contentParagraphs.zh: “肌肉是一种内分泌组织，这意味着当我们运动时，我们的肌肉会释放影响其他身体系统的信号分子，包括促进脑细胞生长和调节新陈代谢，”她说。 “所以肌肉健康就是认知健康。”

contentParagraphs.en: Among those signaling molecules is a protein known as brain-derived neurotrophic factor, or BDNF, which helps regulate glucose and plays an important role in the survival and maintenance of neurons, helping support memory and cognitive function.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这些信号分子中有一种被称为脑源性神经营养因子（BDNF）的蛋白质，它有助于调节葡萄糖，并在神经元的存活和维持中发挥重要作用，有助于支持记忆和认知功能。

contentParagraphs.en: A body's network at work
contentParagraphs.zh: 工作中的身体网络

contentParagraphs.en: Dr. Amit Saini, a geriatrician with Kaiser Permanente in Northern California, says walking, and maintaining the ability to walk well, is a marker of good health because it draws on so many of the body's systems at once. He says walking supports cardiovascular health and lung health.
contentParagraphs.zh: 北加州凯撒医疗机构 (Kaiser Permanente) 的老年病学家阿米特·塞尼 (Amit Saini) 博士表示，步行以及保持良好的步行能力是健康状况良好的标志，因为它同时消耗了身体的许多系统。他说步行有助于心血管健康和肺部健康。

contentParagraphs.en: "As you walk, your heart is beating faster, and when the heart is beating faster, not only is it pumping the blood into the muscle, blood is being pumped to the brain, also into the nerve, and to your other systems," Saini says. "Your lungs are also breathing at a little faster rate, which again is keeping them lighter and healthier."
contentParagraphs.zh: “当你走路时，你的心脏跳动得更快，当心脏跳动得更快时，它不仅将血液泵入肌肉，还将血液泵入大脑、神经和其他系统，”塞尼说。 “你的肺部呼吸速度也加快了，这又让它们变得更轻、更健康。”

contentParagraphs.en: One of the study's more surprising findings: Some super movers showed brain plaques and tangles, which are abnormal proteins associated with Alzheimer's disease and dementia, despite having no symptoms. Researchers say that suggests movement and all the benefits of staying active may help the brain stay resilient even as it undergoes age-related changes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该研究更令人惊讶的发现之一是：一些超级运动者显示出大脑斑块和缠结，这是与阿尔茨海默病和痴呆症相关的异常蛋白质，尽管没有任何症状。研究人员表示，这表明运动和保持活跃的所有好处可能有助于大脑保持弹性，即使它经历了与年龄相关的变化。

contentParagraphs.en: Genetics and lifestyle are also important
contentParagraphs.zh: 遗传和生活方式也很重要

contentParagraphs.en: Genetics likely play a role in who becomes a super mover. A recent study found that genetics accounts for about 50% of a human lifespan, and Milman says among super agers, people who are thriving in their eighties and beyond, the role of genetics may be even greater.
contentParagraphs.zh: 遗传学可能会影响谁成为超级推动者。最近的一项研究发现，基因决定了人类寿命的 50% 左右，米尔曼说，在超级老年人中，也就是八十多岁及以上的人中，基因的作用可能更大。

contentParagraphs.en: Yet the authors emphasize that lifestyle habits, including decisions people make every day about what to eat, prioritizing sleep, taking time to de-stress and gather with friends and family, all matter. In fact, research shows that nearly half of all dementia cases could be prevented or delayed by addressing 14 modifiable risk factors.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然而作者强调，生活习惯，包括人们每天决定吃什么、优先考虑睡眠、花时间减压以及与朋友和家人聚会，都很重要。事实上，研究表明，通过解决 14 种可改变的风险因素，近一半的痴呆症病例可以得到预防或延迟。

contentParagraphs.en: People have agency over improving their odds of healthy aging, and one way to gauge your personal risks, and take steps to decrease your risk, is to assess your Brain Care Score. This is a free, online tool developed by doctors at Massachusetts General Hospital to calculate your risks and take steps, through changes to daily habits, that can help decrease the risk of stroke, dementia, heart disease and cancer.
contentParagraphs.zh: 人们有能力提高健康衰老的几率，衡量个人风险并采取措施降低风险的一种方法是评估您的大脑护理评分。这是一款由马萨诸塞州总医院的医生开发的免费在线工具，用于计算您的风险并通过改变日常习惯采取措施，帮助降低中风、痴呆、心脏病和癌症的风险。

contentParagraphs.en: "Fast walking is a marker that the brain and body are aging well," says Joe Verghese, a researcher and one of the study authors. "But also it's possible that people who walk faster might, by engaging in these activities, also protect their brain health through a variety of mechanisms by reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and promoting brain growth in areas that are essential to maintain cognitive function as you get older."
contentParagraphs.zh: “快走是大脑和身体老化良好的标志，”研究人员、该研究的作者之一乔·韦尔盖斯（Joe Verghese）说。 “但走得更快的人也有可能通过参与这些活动，通过多种机制保护他们的大脑健康，减少炎症，改善心血管健康，并促进大脑在年龄增长时维持认知功能所必需的区域的生长。”

contentParagraphs.en: Verghese says the findings carry a message for people of all ages and fitness levels.
contentParagraphs.zh: 韦尔盖塞说，这些发现对所有年龄段和健康水平的人都传达了一个信息。

contentParagraphs.en: "One of the main messages is, you know, keep mobile," he says. "Exercise regularly, and you know, that might put you on the pathway to being a super mover as you age."
contentParagraphs.zh: “主要信息之一是，你知道，保持移动，”他说。 “定期锻炼，你知道，随着年龄的增长，这可能会让你走上成为超级运动者的道路。”

contentParagraphs.en: Whether it's walking, swimming or cycling, researchers say the form of movement matters less than the consistency. It's a habit that could pay off for both muscle and memory over the long run.
contentParagraphs.zh: 研究人员表示，无论是步行、游泳还是骑自行车，运动的形式比运动的一致性更重要。从长远来看，这是一种对肌肉和记忆力都有好处的习惯。

articles.title: 'I ate ketchup and cheese,' says Venezuelan girl trapped under quake rubble for 32 hours
articles.summary: Fabiana was trapped in the rubble of a 10-storey residential building after two earthquakes rocked Venezuela in June.
articles.titleZh: 被困地震废墟下32小时的委内瑞拉女孩说：“我吃了番茄酱和奶酪”
articles.summaryZh: 六月委内瑞拉发生两次地震后，法比亚娜被困在一栋 10 层住宅楼的废墟中。

contentParagraphs.en: Karina Blanco was just about to start the spinning class she teaches when the earth began to shake. The tremors kept getting stronger, so she grabbed her bag and ran outdoors with everyone else."When I realised the magnitude of it, I started screaming 'my daughter, my daughter'. I sat in my car and drove as fast as I could," said Karina.Her only daughter, Fabiana, 12, was at their home when two powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela within seconds of each other on 24 June. The second quake was one of the strongest tremors to hit the country in a century, at a magnitude of 7.5.When Karina reached her building in Caraballeda, in northern La Guaira state, she could hardly believe her eyes. "I could see one building, then a gap where my building stood, and then another building."Inside their first-floor flat in the 10-storey building, Fabiana was in her mother's bedroom when she felt the earthquakes. She ran into the kitchen, and was holding on to the counter, when the walls around her collapsed. She was thrown to the ground."I saw things shaking, falling, breaking, and then the walls cracked. The wall separating my apartment from a friend's collapsed. At that moment, I thought, 'I'm going to die. I won't survive this. No-one is going to rescue me,'" said Fabiana.From then began an excruciating 32 hours.Outside the collapsed building, Karina saw half of her daughter's bed sticking out of the debris."I was running from one end of the complex to the other screaming 'She's dead. My daughter is dead'. I didn't know what to do," said Karina.Under the collapsed building everything had gone quiet for Fabiana. She was lying face up, trapped by rubble on all sides, with the ceiling almost touching her face."I'm someone who gets very anxious and claustrophobic. But I don't know why, a strange calm came over me. Maybe my mind was in shock," she said.A little while later, a nurse who worked as a carer for her upstairs neighbours started calling out to see if anyone could hear her. Fabiana responded."She told me to stay calm and that everything would be alright," said Fabiana.Six hours after the earthquake, at around midnight, the nurse was rescued. She told the volunteers who pulled her out that a girl named Fabiana was alive inside."I had surrendered to God asking for strength to begin a new life without Fabiana. And then someone told me, 'Your daughter is alive'," said Karina.She ran back to the building screaming into gaps in the debris, calling out her daughter's name.Through the pile of rubble, Fabiana could hear nothing."For some reason, I had hope and faith," she said. "One of my legs was bent in a painful position, and I moved some of the rubble so I could straighten it out. While doing that I got scrapes and cuts, but I found a bottle of ketchup and some grated cheese. That's what kept me conscious."At dawn a group of Venezuelan firefighters came to the building. They went into the rubble and called out for Fabiana, but they heard nothing in response.It was one of many moments that Karina swung from hope to despair."They told me nothing could be done, and they left. I had a sinking thought that maybe she had suffocated to death or had suffered a heart attack. Then a volunteer came up to me and asked me what was going on. He - Viktor - was my hero," she said.Under the rubble Fabiana found her phone. There was no signal as mobile phone networks had gone down, but she decided to record a video of herself. She thought she would eventually be able to send it to her mother or someone who could help."Apartment - Ritamar Palace. There was a tremor and lot of rubble has fallen. There is no light. There is no-one to rescue us. I am alone. Many neighbours are trapped in the rubble. We need your help," Fabiana can be seen saying in the video.Meanwhile Viktor had clambered up the debris and began calling out to Fabiana. This time she heard him and replied. He told Karina."I turned to everyone and screamed, 'my daughter is alive'," said Karina. "People started arriving in droves, they started bringing tools. But the firefighters who were there said it was impossible to get through, and they left."A few hours later another group of firefighters arrived. They assured her they would bring Fabiana out. But they couldn't manage to reach her, either.Meanwhile, Viktor - the volunteer - kept going back to the spot from where he could speak to Fabiana to reassure her.The firefighters called for a rescue group from Caracas - but by the time they arrived, it had become dark.Karina ran around looking for torches and begged people to help. Seven motorcycles and a couple of cars pointed their headlights at the collapsed building.Bit by bit they chiselled away, and finally they made a hole large enough that they could see Fabiana.The video of this moment - of a smiling Fabiana peering through the hole - has gone viral in Venezuela."After so many hours of being shut in, I was filled with joy when I saw them. I realised I was going to be rescued," said Fabiana.At around 02:00 local time on Friday - 32 hours after the earthquakes struck - they managed to dig a tunnel wide enough to pull Fabiana out. She walked out of the debris with the support of the rescuers and collapsed into the arms of her mother."When I came out, I saw my family, I saw the building completely collapsed, and it felt like it wasn't real, like it was a TV series," said Fabiana.Karina says that of the nearly 50 people who lived in her building, only three were rescued alive.As of Sunday, 3,342 people were confirmed to have died in the quakes, with tens of thousands still missing.Apart from a fracture in her left foot and a few scrapes and bruises, Fabiana suffered no other injuries.She is now living with her grandmother."Initially I was scared to lie down, especially on my back, as I would remember the time I spent in the rubble," she said.In the streets just outside their current home in La Guaira, there are many collapsed buildings."There is a great sadnes
contentParagraphs.zh: 卡琳娜·布兰科 (Karina Blanco) 正要开始她所教的动感单车课程时，大地开始震动。震动越来越强烈，她抓起包，和其他人一起跑到户外。卡琳娜说：“当我意识到地震的严重性时，我开始尖叫‘我的女儿，我的女儿’。我坐在车里，尽可能快地开车。”6月24日，当委内瑞拉发生两次强烈地震时，她唯一的女儿、12岁的法比安娜正在家里。第二次地震是该国一个世纪以来最强烈的地震之一，震级为 7.5 级。当卡琳娜到达位于拉瓜伊拉州北部卡拉巴莱达的大楼时，她简直不敢相信自己的眼睛。 “我可以看到一栋建筑，然后是我的建筑所在的缺口，然后是另一栋建筑。” 在这栋 10 层建筑的一楼公寓内，法比亚娜感到地震时正在她母亲的卧室里。她跑进厨房，抓住柜台，这时她周围的墙壁倒塌了。她被扔到了地上。法比亚娜说：“我看到东西摇晃、倒塌、破碎，然后墙壁裂开了。分隔我和朋友公寓的墙倒塌了。那一刻，我想，‘我要死了。我活不下去了。没有人能救我。’”从那时起，痛苦的32小时开始了。在倒塌的建筑物外，卡琳娜看到她女儿的床的一半从废墟中伸出来。从建筑群的一端到另一端都在尖叫着‘我女儿死了’，我不知道该怎么办，”卡琳娜说。在倒塌的建筑下，一切都变得安静了。她脸朝上躺着，四面都被瓦砾困住，天花板几乎碰到了她的脸。“我是一个非常焦虑和患有幽闭恐惧症的人。但我不知道为什么，一种奇怪的平静笼罩着我。也许我的心很震惊，”她说。过了一会儿，一名为楼上邻居做护理的护士开始大声喊叫，看看是否有人能听到她的声音。法比亚娜回应道。“她告诉我要保持冷静，一切都会好起来的，”法比亚娜说。地震发生六小时后，午夜左右，护士获救。她告诉把她救出来的志愿者，里面还活着一个名叫法比亚娜的女孩。卡琳娜说：“我向上帝投降，请求力量在没有法比亚娜的情况下开始新的生活。然后有人告诉我，‘你的女儿还活着’。”她跑回大楼，在废墟的缝隙中尖叫着，呼喊着她女儿的名字。透过一堆瓦砾，法比亚娜什么也听不到。“出于某种原因，我有希望和信念，”她说。 “我的一条腿弯曲得很痛苦，我搬动了一些瓦砾，这样我就可以把它弄直。在这样做的过程中，我被擦伤和割伤，但我发现了一瓶番茄酱和一些磨碎的奶酪。这就是让我保持清醒的原因。”黎明时分，一群委内瑞拉消防员来到了大楼。他们走进废墟，呼唤法比亚娜，但没有听到任何回应。这是卡琳娜从希望转向绝望的众多时刻之一。“他们告诉我无能为力，然后他们就离开了。我突然想到，也许她窒息而死或者心脏病发作。然后一名志愿者走到我身边，问我发生了什么事。他——维克托——是我的英雄。”她说。法比亚娜在废墟下找到了她的手机。由于手机网络中断，没有信号，但她决定录制自己的视频。她认为她最终能够将它寄给她的母亲或可以提供帮助的人。视频中可以看到法比亚娜说道：“公寓——丽塔玛宫。发生了地震，很多瓦砾倒塌了。没有光。没有人来救我们。我独自一人。许多邻居被困在瓦砾中。我们需要你的帮助。”与此同时，维克多爬上了废墟，开始向法比亚娜呼喊。这次她听到了他的话并回答了。他告诉卡琳娜。“我转向每个人尖叫，‘我的女儿还活着’，”卡琳娜说。 “人们开始成群结队地到达，他们开始携带工具。但在那里的消防员说不可能通过，然后他们就离开了。” 几个小时后，另一组消防员到达了。他们向她保证他们会把法比亚娜带出来。但他们也无法联系到她。与此同时，志愿者维克多不断回到可以与法比亚娜交谈的地方，以安抚她。消防员呼叫了来自加拉加斯的救援队，但当他们到达时，天已经黑了。卡琳娜到处寻找火把并请求人们帮助。七辆摩托车和几辆汽车将车头灯对准了倒塌的建筑物。他们一点一点地凿开，最后凿出了一个足够大的洞，足以看到法比亚娜。这一刻的视频——法比亚娜微笑着从洞里往外看——在委内瑞拉疯传。法比亚娜说：“在被关了几个小时后，当我看到他们时，我充满了喜悦。我意识到我要获救了。”当地时间周五02:00左右- 地震发生 32 小时后 - 他们设法挖了一条足够宽的隧道，将法比亚娜救了出来。她在救援人员的搀扶下走出废墟，倒在母亲的怀里。法比亚娜说：“当我出来时，我看到了我的家人，我看到大楼完全倒塌了，感觉不太真实，就像是电视剧一样。”卡琳娜说，住在她大楼里的近50人中，只有三人生还。截至周日，已确认有3342人在地震中死亡，其中有数万人。至今仍下落不明。除了左脚骨折和几处擦伤和擦伤外，法比亚娜没有受到其他伤害。她现在和祖母住在一起。“一开始我很害怕躺下，尤其是仰卧，因为我会记得在废墟中度过的时光，”她说。在他们现在位于拉瓜伊拉的家外的街道上，有许多倒塌的建筑物。
